<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--drop-default-sharding-strategy"></a>

# DROP DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY` syntax is used to drop default sharding strategy for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropDefaultShardingStrategy ::=
  'DROP' 'DEFAULT' 'SHARDING' ('TABLE' | 'DATABASE') 'STRATEGY' ifExists? ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Default sharding strategy not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop default sharding table strategy for specified database

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHARDING TABLE STRATEGY FROM sharding_db;
```

- Drop default sharding database strategy for current database

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHARDING DATABASE STRATEGY;
```

- Drop default sharding table strategy with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHARDING TABLE STRATEGY IF EXISTS;
```

- Drop default sharding database strategy with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHARDING DATABASE STRATEGY IF EXISTS;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `DEFAULT` , `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `DATABASE` ,`STRATEGY`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-default-sharding-strategy--related-links"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--drop-sharding-key-generator"></a>

# DROP SHARDING KEY GENERATOR

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP SHARDING KEY GENERATOR` syntax is used to drop sharding key generator for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropShardingKeyGenerator ::=
  'DROP' 'SHARDING' 'KEY' 'GENERATOR' ifExists? keyGeneratorName (keyGeneratorName)* ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

keyGeneratorName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Sharding key generator not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop sharding key generator for specified database

```sql
DROP SHARDING KEY GENERATOR t_order_snowflake FROM sharding_db;
```

- Drop sharding key generator for current database

```sql
DROP SHARDING KEY GENERATOR t_order_snowflake;
```

- Drop sharding key generator with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP SHARDING KEY GENERATOR IF EXISTS t_order_snowflake;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `SHARDING`, `KEY`, `GENERATOR`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-key-generator--related-links"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--drop-sharding-algorithm"></a>

# DROP SHARDING ALGORITHM

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP SHARDING ALGORITHM` syntax is used to drop sharding algorithm for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropShardingAlgorithm ::=
  'DROP' 'SHARDING' 'ALGORITHM' algorithmName ifExists? ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

algorithmName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause used for avoid `Sharding algorithm not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop sharding algorithm for specified database

```sql
DROP SHARDING ALGORITHM t_order_hash_mod FROM sharding_db;
```

- Drop sharding algorithm for current database

```sql
DROP SHARDING ALGORITHM t_order_hash_mod;
```

- Drop sharding algorithm with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP SHARDING ALGORITHM IF EXISTS t_order_hash_mod;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `SHARDING`, `ALGORITHM`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--drop-sharding-table-reference-rule"></a>

# DROP SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE` syntax is used to drop specified sharding table reference rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropShardingTableReferenceRule ::=
  'DROP' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'REFERENCE' 'RULE' ifExists? ruleName (',' ruleName)*

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

ruleName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Sharding reference rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop a specified sharding table reference rule

```sql
DROP SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE ref_0;
```

- Drop multiple sharding table reference rules

```sql
DROP SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE ref_0, ref_1;
```

- Drop sharding table reference rule with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE IF EXISTS ref_0;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-sharding-drop-sharding-table-reference-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `REFERENCE`, `RULE`

  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--supplement)

  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `CREATE BROADCAST TABLE RULE` syntax is used to create broadcast table rules for tables that need to be
broadcast (broadcast tables)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CreateBroadcastTableRule ::=
  'CREATE' 'BROADCAST' 'TABLE' 'RULE' ifNotExists? tableName (',' tableName)* 

ifNotExists ::=
  'IF' 'NOT' 'EXISTS'

tableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `tableName` can use an existing table or a table that will be created;
- `ifNotExists` clause is used for avoid `Duplicate Broadcast rule` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--example"></a>

### Example

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--create-broadcast-table-rule-2"></a>

#### Create broadcast table rule

```sql
-- Add t_province, t_city to broadcast table rules
CREATE BROADCAST TABLE RULE t_province, t_city;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--create-broadcast-table-rule-with-ifnotexists-clause"></a>

#### Create broadcast table rule with `ifNotExists` clause

```sql
CREATE BROADCAST TABLE RULE IF NOT EXISTS t_province, t_city;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`CREATE`, `BROADCAST`, `TABLE`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-create-broadcast-table-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

DROP BROADCAST TABLE RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--drop-broadcast-table-rule"></a>

# DROP BROADCAST TABLE RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP BROADCAST TABLE RULE` syntax is used to drop broadcast table rule for specified broadcast tables

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropBroadcastTableRule ::=
  'DROP' 'BROADCAST' 'TABLE' 'RULE' ifExists? tableName (',' tableName)* 

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

tableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `tableName` can use the table of existing broadcast rules;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Broadcast rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop broadcast table rule for specified broadcast table

```sql
DROP BROADCAST TABLE RULE t_province, t_city;
```

- Drop broadcast table rule with `ifExists` clause

```
DROP BROADCAST TABLE RULE IF EXISTS t_province, t_city;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `BROADCAST`, `TABLE`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-broadcast-table-drop-broadcast-table-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<!-- page_index: 144 -->

# LOAD SINGLE TABLE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rdl/rule-definition/single-table/load-single-table.en.md "Edit this page")

LOAD SINGLE TABLE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--load-single-table"></a>

# LOAD SINGLE TABLE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--description"></a>

## Description

The `LOAD SINGLE TABLE` syntax is used to load single table from storage unit.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
loadSingleTable ::=
  'LOAD' 'SINGLE' 'TABLE' tableDefinition

tableDefinition ::=
  tableIdentifier (',' tableIdentifier)*

tableIdentifier ::=
  '*.*' | '*.*.*' | storageUnitName '.*' | storageUnitName '.*.*' | storageUnitName '.' schemaName '.*' | storageUnitName '.' tableName | storageUnitName '.' schemaName '.' tableName

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier

schemaName ::=
  identifier

tableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- support specifying schemaName in PostgreSQL and OpenGauss protocols

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--example"></a>

### Example

- Load specified single table

```sql
LOAD SINGLE TABLE ds_0.t_single;
```

- Load all single tables in the specified storage unit

```sql
LOAD SINGLE TABLE ds_0.*;
```

- Load all single tables

```sql
LOAD SINGLE TABLE *.*;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`LOAD`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-load-single-table--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table"></a>

<!-- page_index: 145 -->

# UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE

UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--unload-single-table"></a>

# UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--description"></a>

## Description

The `UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE` syntax is used to unload single table.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
unloadSingleTable ::=
  'UNLOAD' 'SINGLE' 'TABLE' tableNames

tableNames ::=
  tableName (',' tableName)*

tableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- Unlike loading, only the table name needs to be specified when unloading a single table

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--example"></a>

### Example

- Unload specified single table

```sql
UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE t_single;
```

- Load all single tables

```sql
UNLOAD SINGLE TABLE *;
-- or
UNLOAD ALL SINGLE TABLES;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`UNLOAD`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`, `ALL`, `TABLES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-unload-single-table--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<!-- page_index: 146 -->

# SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rdl/rule-definition/single-table/set-default-single-table-storage-unit.en.md "Edit this page")

SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--set-default-single-table-storage-unit"></a>

# SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--description"></a>

## Description

The `SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT` syntax is used to set default single table storage unit.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
SetDefaultSingleTableStorageUnit ::=
  'SET' 'DEFAULT' 'SINGLE' 'TABLE' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' singleTableDefinition

singleTableDefinition ::=
  '=' (storageUnitName | 'RANDOM')

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `STORAGE UNIT` needs to use storage unit managed by RDL. The `RANDOM` keyword stands for random storage.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--example"></a>

### Example

- Set a default single table storage unit

```sql
SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT = ds_0;
```

- Set the default single table storage unit to random storage

```sql
SET DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT = RANDOM;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SET`, `DEFAULT`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`, `RANDOM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-single-table-set-default-single-table-storage-unit--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

Retro

Haitian

Deep

Dark

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rdl/rule-definition/readwrite-splitting/drop-readwrite-splitting-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

DROP READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--drop-readwrite_splitting-rule"></a>

# DROP READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE` syntax is used to drop readwrite-splitting rule for specified database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropReadwriteSplittingRule ::=
  'DROP' 'READWRITE_SPLITTING' 'RULE' ifExists? ruleName (',' ruleName)* ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

ruleName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Readwrite-splitting rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop readwrite-splitting rule for specified database

```sql
DROP READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_1 FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
```

- Drop readwrite-splitting rule for current database

```sql
DROP READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_1;
```

- Drop readwrite-splitting rule with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE IF EXISTS ms_group_1;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-readwrite-splitting-drop-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# Encrypt :: ShardingSphere

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--drop-encrypt-rule"></a>

# DROP ENCRYPT RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--example)
  - [Reserved words](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--reserved-words)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP ENCRYPT RULE` syntax is used to drop an existing encryption rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropEncryptRule ::=
  'DROP' 'ENCRYPT' 'RULE' ifExists? ruleName (',' ruleName)*

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

ruleName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Encrypt rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop an encrypt rule

```sql
DROP ENCRYPT RULE t_encrypt, t_encrypt_2;
```

- Drop encrypt with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP ENCRYPT RULE IF EXISTS t_encrypt, t_encrypt_2;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--reserved-words"></a>

### Reserved words

`DROP`, `ENCRYPT`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-encrypt-drop-encrypt-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-mask-drop-mask-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP MASK RULE` syntax is used to drop existing mask rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-mask-drop-mask-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropMaskRule ::=
  'DROP' 'MASK' 'RULE' ifExists? ruleName (',' ruleName)*

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

ruleName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-mask-drop-mask-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `ifExists` clause used for avoid `Mask rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-mask-drop-mask-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop mask rule

```sql
DROP MASK RULE t_mask, t_mask_1;
```

- Drop mask rule with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP MASK RULE IF EXISTS t_mask, t_mask_1;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-mask-drop-mask-rule--reserved-words"></a>

### Reserved words

`DROP`, `MASK`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--drop-shadow-rule"></a>

# DROP SHADOW RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP SHADOW RULE` syntax is used to drop shadow rule for specified database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropShadowRule ::=
  'DROP' 'SHADOW' 'RULE' ifExists? ruleName ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

ruleName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `Shadow rule not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop shadow rule for specified database

```sql
DROP SHADOW RULE shadow_rule FROM shadow_db;
```

- Drop shadow rule for current database

```sql
DROP SHADOW RULE shadow_rule;
```

- Drop shadow rule with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP SHADOW RULE IF EXISTS shadow_rule;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `SHODOW`, `RULE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

CREATE DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--create-default-shadow-algorithm"></a>

# CREATE DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

## Description

The `CREATE DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM` syntax is used to create a default shadow algorithm.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CreateDefaultShadowAlgorithm ::=
  'CREATE' 'DEFAULT' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHM' ifNotExists? shadowAlgorithm 

ifNotExists ::=
  'IF' 'NOT' 'EXISTS'

shadowAlgorithm ::=
  'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' algorithmType ',' propertiesDefiinition ')'
    
algorithmType ::=
  string

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' key '=' value (',' key '=' value)* ')'

key ::=
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `algorithmType` currently supports `VALUE_MATCH`, `REGEX_MATCH` and `SQL_HINT`;
- `ifNotExists` clause is used for avoid `Duplicate default shadow algorithm` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Create default shadow algorithm

```sql
CREATE DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT");
```

- Create default shadow algorithm with `ifNotExist` clause

```sql
CREATE DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM IF NOT EXISTS TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT");
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`CREATE`, `DEFAULT`, `SHADOW`, `ALGORITHM`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-create-default-shadow-algorithm--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

## Description

The `ALTER DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM` syntax is used to alter a default shadow algorithm.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterDefaultShadowAlgorithm ::=
  'ALTER' 'DEFAULT' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHM' shadowAlgorithm 

shadowAlgorithm ::=
  'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' algorithmType ',' propertiesDefiinition ')'
    
algorithmType ::=
  string

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' key '=' value (',' key '=' value)* ')'

key ::=
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `algorithmType` currently supports `VALUE_MATCH`, `REGEX_MATCH` and `SQL_HINT`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter default shadow algorithm

```sql
ALTER DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT");
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `DEFAULT`, `SHADOW`, `ALGORITHM`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-alter-default-shadow-algorithm--related-links"></a>

  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM` syntax is used to drop default shadow algorithm for specified database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropDefaultShadowAlgorithm ::=
  'DROP' 'DEFAULT' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHM' ifExists? ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause used for avoid `Default shadow algorithm not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop default shadow algorithm for specified database

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM FROM shadow_db;
```

- Drop default shadow algorithm for current database

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM;
```

- Drop default shadow algorithm with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM IF EXISTS;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `DEFAULT`, `SHODOW`, `ALGORITHM`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-default-shadow-algorithm--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<!-- page_index: 166 -->

# DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rdl/rule-definition/shadow/drop-shadow-algorithm.en.md "Edit this page")

DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--drop-shadow-algorithm"></a>

# DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

## Description

The `DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM` syntax is used to drop shadow algorithm for specified database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
DropShadowAlgorithm ::=
  'DROP' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHM' ifExists? algorithmName (',' algorithmName)* ('FROM' databaseName)?

ifExists ::=
  'IF' 'EXISTS'

algorithmName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted;
- `ifExists` clause is used for avoid `shadow algorithm not exists` error.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop mutiple shadow algorithm for specified database

```sql
DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM shadow_rule_t_order_sql_hint_0, shadow_rule_t_order_item_sql_hint_0 FROM shadow_db;
```

- Drop single shadow algorithm for current database

```sql
DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM shadow_rule_t_order_sql_hint_0;
```

- Drop shadow algorithm with `ifExists` clause

```sql
DROP SHADOW ALGORITHM IF EXISTS shadow_rule_t_order_sql_hint_0;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`DROP`, `SHODOW`, `ALGORITHM`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rdl-rule-definition-shadow-drop-shadow-algorithm--related-links"></a>

### Related links

# SHOW STORAGE UNITS

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/storage-unit-query/show-storage-units.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW STORAGE UNITS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--show-storage-units"></a>

# SHOW STORAGE UNITS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW STORAGE UNITS` syntax is used to query the storage units that have been added to the specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowStorageUnit ::=
  'SHOW' 'STORAGE' 'UNITS' ('FROM' databaseName)? showLike?

databaseName ::=
  identifier

showLike ::=
  'LIKE' likePattern

likePattern ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`; if `DATABASE` is not used, it will prompt `No database selected`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Storage unit name |
| type | Storage unit type |
| host | Storage unit host |
| port | Storage unit port |
| db | Database name |
| connection\_timeout\_milliseconds | connection timeout (milliseconds) |
| idle\_timeout\_milliseconds | idle timeout (milliseconds) |
| max\_lifetime\_milliseconds | max lifetime (milliseconds) |
| max\_pool\_size | max pool size |
| min\_pool\_size | min pool size |
| read\_only | read-only flag |
| other\_attributes | other attributes |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--example"></a>

### Example

- Query storage units from current database

```sql
mysql> SHOW STORAGE UNITS; +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | name | type  | host      | port | db   | connection_timeout_milliseconds | idle_timeout_milliseconds | max_lifetime_milliseconds | max_pool_size | min_pool_size | read_only | other_attributes                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | ds_1 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | db1  | 30000                           | 60000                     | 2100000                   | 50            | 1             | false     | {"healthCheckProperties":{},"initializationFailTimeout":1,"validationTimeout":5000,"keepaliveTime":0,"leakDetectionThreshold":0,"registerMbeans":false,"allowPoolSuspension":false,"autoCommit":true,"isolateInternalQueries":false} | | ds_0 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | db0  | 30000                           | 60000                     | 2100000                   | 50            | 1             | false     | {"healthCheckProperties":{},"initializationFailTimeout":1,"validationTimeout":5000,"keepaliveTime":0,"leakDetectionThreshold":0,"registerMbeans":false,"allowPoolSuspension":false,"autoCommit":true,"isolateInternalQueries":false} | +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query storage units from specified database

```sql
mysql> SHOW STORAGE UNITS FROM sharding_db; +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | name | type  | host      | port | db   | connection_timeout_milliseconds | idle_timeout_milliseconds | max_lifetime_milliseconds | max_pool_size | min_pool_size | read_only | other_attributes                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | ds_1 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | db1  | 30000                           | 60000                     | 2100000                   | 50            | 1             | false     | {"healthCheckProperties":{},"initializationFailTimeout":1,"validationTimeout":5000,"keepaliveTime":0,"leakDetectionThreshold":0,"registerMbeans":false,"allowPoolSuspension":false,"autoCommit":true,"isolateInternalQueries":false} | | ds_0 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | db0  | 30000                           | 60000                     | 2100000                   | 50            | 1             | false     | {"healthCheckProperties":{},"initializationFailTimeout":1,"validationTimeout":5000,"keepaliveTime":0,"leakDetectionThreshold":0,"registerMbeans":false,"allowPoolSuspension":false,"autoCommit":true,"isolateInternalQueries":false} | +------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query storage units with like clause

```sql
mysql> SHOW STORAGE UNITS LIKE '%_0';
+------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| name | type  | host      | port | db   | connection_timeout_milliseconds | idle_timeout_milliseconds | max_lifetime_milliseconds | max_pool_size | min_pool_size | read_only | other_attributes                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
+------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| ds_0 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | db0  | 30000                           | 60000                     | 2100000                   | 50            | 1             | false     | {"healthCheckProperties":{},"initializationFailTimeout":1,"validationTimeout":5000,"keepaliveTime":0,"leakDetectionThreshold":0,"registerMbeans":false,"allowPoolSuspension":false,"autoCommit":true,"isolateInternalQueries":false} |
+------+-------+-----------+------+------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `STORAGE`, `UNITS`, `FROM`, `LIKE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-storage-unit-query-show-storage-units--related-links"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/sharding/show-sharding-table-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--show-sharding-table-rule"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULE` syntax is used to query the sharding table rule in the specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingTableRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' ('RULE' tableName | 'RULES') ('FROM' databaseName)?

tableName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| table | Logical table name |
| actual\_data\_nodes | Actual data node |
| actual\_data\_sources | Actual data source (Displayed when creating rules by RDL) |
| database\_strategy\_type | Database sharding strategy type |
| database\_sharding\_column | Database sharding column |
| database\_sharding\_algorithm\_type | Database sharding algorithm type |
| database\_sharding\_algorithm\_props | Database sharding algorithm properties |
| table\_strategy\_type | Table sharding strategy type |
| table\_sharding\_column | Table sharding column |
| table\_sharding\_algorithm\_type | Table sharding algorithm type |
| table\_sharding\_algorithm\_props | Table sharding algorithm properties |
| key\_generate\_column | Sharding key generator column |
| key\_generator\_type | Sharding key generator type |
| key\_generator\_props | Sharding key generator properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the sharding table rules of the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| table        | actual_data_nodes | actual_data_sources | database_strategy_type | database_sharding_column | database_sharding_algorithm_type | database_sharding_algorithm_props | table_strategy_type | table_sharding_column | table_sharding_algorithm_type | table_sharding_algorithm_props | key_generate_column | key_generator_type | key_generator_props |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| t_order      |                   | ds_0,ds_1           |                        |                          |                                  |                                   | mod                 | order_id              | mod                           | sharding-count=4               |                     |                    |                     |
| t_order_item |                   | ds_0,ds_1           |                        |                          |                                  |                                   | mod                 | order_id              | mod                           | sharding-count=4               |                     |                    |                     |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
2 rows in set (0.12 sec)
```

- Query the sharding table rules of the current logic database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES;
```

```sql
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| table        | actual_data_nodes | actual_data_sources | database_strategy_type | database_sharding_column | database_sharding_algorithm_type | database_sharding_algorithm_props | table_strategy_type | table_sharding_column | table_sharding_algorithm_type | table_sharding_algorithm_props | key_generate_column | key_generator_type | key_generator_props |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| t_order      |                   | ds_0,ds_1           |                        |                          |                                  |                                   | mod                 | order_id              | mod                           | sharding-count=4               |                     |                    |                     |
| t_order_item |                   | ds_0,ds_1           |                        |                          |                                  |                                   | mod                 | order_id              | mod                           | sharding-count=4               |                     |                    |                     |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
2 rows in set (0.12 sec)
```

- Query the specified sharding table rule

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULE t_order;
```

```sql
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| table        | actual_data_nodes | actual_data_sources | database_strategy_type | database_sharding_column | database_sharding_algorithm_type | database_sharding_algorithm_props | table_strategy_type | table_sharding_column | table_sharding_algorithm_type | table_sharding_algorithm_props | key_generate_column | key_generator_type | key_generator_props |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| t_order      |                   | ds_0,ds_1           |                        |                          |                                  |                                   | mod                 | order_id              | mod                           | sharding-count=4               |                     |                    |                     |
+--------------+-------------------+---------------------+------------------------+--------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------------------------+---------------------+-----------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------------+---------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.12 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `RULE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--show-sharding-algorithms"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS` syntax is used to query the sharding algorithms in the specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingAlgorithms::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'ALGORITHMS' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding algorithm name |
| type | Sharding algorithm type |
| props | Sharding algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the sharding table algorithms of the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS FROM sharding_db; +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | name                    | type   | props                                               | +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | t_order_inline          | INLINE | algorithm-expression=t_order_${order_id % 2}        | | t_order_item_inline     | INLINE | algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}   | +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query the sharding table algorithms of the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHMS; +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | name                    | type   | props                                               | +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | t_order_inline          | INLINE | algorithm-expression=t_order_${order_id % 2}        | | t_order_item_inline     | INLINE | algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}   | +-------------------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `ALGORITHMS`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-algorithms--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

SHOW UNUSED SHARDING ALGORITHMS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--show-unused-sharding-algorithms"></a>

# SHOW UNUSED SHARDING ALGORITHMS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW UNUSED SHARDING ALGORITHMS` syntax is used to query the unused sharding algorithms in the specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingAlgorithms::=
  'SHOW' 'UNUSED' 'SHARDING' 'ALGORITHMS' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding algorithm name |
| type | Sharding algorithm type |
| props | Sharding algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the unused sharding table algorithms of the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW UNUSED SHARDING ALGORITHMS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNUSED SHARDING ALGORITHMS;
+---------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| name          | type   | props                                               |
+---------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+
| t1_inline     | INLINE | algorithm-expression=t_order_${order_id % 2}        |
+---------------+--------+-----------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `UNUSED`, `SHARDING`, `ALGORITHMS`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-algorithms--related-links"></a>

# SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/sharding/show-default-sharding-strategy.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--show-default-sharding-strategy"></a>

# SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY` syntax is used to query default sharding strategy in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowDefaultShardingStrategy::=
  'SHOW' 'DEFAULT' 'SHARDING' 'STRATEGY' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding strategy scope |
| type | Sharding strategy type |
| sharding\_column | Sharding column |
| sharding\_algorithm\_name | Sharding algorithm name |
| sharding\_algorithm\_type | Sharding algorithm type |
| sharding\_algorithm\_props | Sharding algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--example"></a>

### Example

- Query default sharding strategy in specified database.

```sql
SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY FROM sharding_db; +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | name     | type     | sharding_column | sharding_algorithm_name | sharding_algorithm_type | sharding_algorithm_props                            | +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | TABLE    | STANDARD | order_id        | table_inline            | inline                  | {algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}} | | DATABASE | STANDARD | order_id        | table_inline            | inline                  | {algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}} | +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query default sharding strategy in current database.

```sql
SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DEFAULT SHARDING STRATEGY; +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | name     | type     | sharding_column | sharding_algorithm_name | sharding_algorithm_type | sharding_algorithm_props                            | +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ | TABLE    | STANDARD | order_id        | table_inline            | inline                  | {algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}} | | DATABASE | STANDARD | order_id        | table_inline            | inline                  | {algorithm-expression=t_order_item_${order_id % 2}} | +----------+----------+-----------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `DEFAULT`, `SHARDING`, `STRATEGY`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-default-sharding-strategy--related-links"></a>

### Related links
