[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/sharding/show-unused-sharding-key-generators.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--show-unused-sharding-key-generators"></a>

# SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS` syntax is used to query sharding key generators that are not used in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowUnusedShardingKeyGenerators::=
  'SHOW' 'UNUSED' 'SHARDING' 'KEY' 'GENERATOR' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding key generator name |
| type | Sharding key generator type |
| props | Sharding key generator properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding key generators that are not used in the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS FROM sharding_db;
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
| name                    | type      | props |
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
| snowflake_key_generator | snowflake |       |
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding key generators that are not used in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNUSED SHARDING KEY GENERATORS;
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
| name                    | type      | props |
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
| snowflake_key_generator | snowflake |       |
+-------------------------+-----------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `UNUSED`, `SHARDING`, `KEY`, `GENERATORS`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-key-generators--related-links"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--show-sharding-auditors"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS` syntax is used to query sharding auditors in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingAuditors::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'AUDITORS' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding auditor name |
| type | Sharding auditor algorithm type |
| props | Sharding auditor algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding auditors for the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS FROM sharding_db;
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| name                          | type                    | props |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| sharding_key_required_auditor | dml_sharding_conditions |       |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query sharding auditors for the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS;
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| name                          | type                    | props |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| sharding_key_required_auditor | dml_sharding_conditions |       |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-auditors--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `AUDITORS`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--show-unused-sharding-auditors"></a>

# SHOW UNUSED SHARDING AUDITORS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING AUDITORS` syntax is used to query sharding auditors that are not used in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowUnusedShardingAuditors::=
  'SHOW' 'UNUSED' 'SHARDING' 'AUDITOR' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding auditor name |
| type | Sharding auditor algorithm type |
| props | Sharding auditor algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding auditors that are not used in the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW UNUSED SHARDING AUDITORS FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNUSED SHARDING AUDITORS FROM sharding_db;
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| name                          | type                    | props |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| sharding_key_required_auditor | dml_sharding_conditions |       |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query sharding auditors are not used in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW UNUSED SHARDING AUDITORS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNUSED SHARDING AUDITORS;
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| name                          | type                    | props |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
| sharding_key_required_auditor | dml_sharding_conditions |       |
+-------------------------------+-------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `UNUSED`, `SHARDING`, `AUDITORS`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-unused-sharding-auditors--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES

Deep

SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--show-sharding-table-nodes"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES` syntax is used to query sharding table nodes in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingTableNode::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'NODES' tableName? ('FROM' databaseName)?

tableName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Columns | Descriptions |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding rule name |
| nodes | Sharding nodes |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding table nodes for specified table in the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES t_order_item FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES t_order_item FROM sharding_db;
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| name         | nodes                                                                                                      |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| t_order_item | resource_0.t_order_item_0, resource_0.t_order_item_1, resource_1.t_order_item_0, resource_1.t_order_item_1 |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table nodes for specified table in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES t_order_item;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES t_order_item;
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| name         | nodes                                                                                                      |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| t_order_item | resource_0.t_order_item_0, resource_0.t_order_item_1, resource_1.t_order_item_0, resource_1.t_order_item_1 |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec
```

- Query sharding table nodes for all tables in the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES FROM sharding_db;
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| name         | nodes                                                                                                      |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| t_order_item | resource_0.t_order_item_0, resource_0.t_order_item_1, resource_1.t_order_item_0, resource_1.t_order_item_1 |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table nodes for all tables in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE NODES;
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| name         | nodes                                                                                                      |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| t_order_item | resource_0.t_order_item_0, resource_0.t_order_item_1, resource_1.t_order_item_0, resource_1.t_order_item_1 |
+--------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `NODES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-nodes--related-links"></a>

SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--syntax)

  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM` syntax is used to query sharding rules used specified sharding algorithm in specified logical database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingTableRulesUsedAlgorithm::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'RULES' 'USED' 'ALGORITHM' algorithmName ('FROM' databaseName)?

algorithmName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Columns | Descriptions |
| --- | --- |
| type | Sharding rule type |
| name | Sharding rule name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding table rules for the specified sharding algorithm in spicified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM table_inline FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM table_inline FROM sharding_db;
+-------+--------------+
| type  | name         |
+-------+--------------+
| table | t_order_item |
+-------+--------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table rules for specified sharding algorithm in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM table_inline;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED ALGORITHM table_inline;
+-------+--------------+
| type  | name         |
+-------+--------------+
| table | t_order_item |
+-------+--------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `RULES`, `USED`, `ALGORITHM`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-algorithm--related-links"></a>

SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--description)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR` syntax is used to query sharding rules used specified sharding key generator in specified logical database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingTableRulesUsedKeyGenerator::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'RULES' 'USED' 'KEY' 'GENERATOR' keyGeneratorName ('FROM' databaseName)?

keyGeneratorName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Columns | Descriptions |
| --- | --- |
| type | Sharding rule type |
| name | Sharding rule name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding table rules for the specified sharding key generator in spicified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR snowflake_key_generator FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR snowflake_key_generator FROM sharding_db;
+-------+--------------+
| type  | name         |
+-------+--------------+
| table | t_order_item |
+-------+--------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table rules for specified sharding key generator in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR snowflake_key_generator;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED KEY GENERATOR snowflake_key_generator;
+-------+--------------+
| type  | name         |
+-------+--------------+
| table | t_order_item |
+-------+--------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-key-generator--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `USED`, `KEY`, `GENERATOR`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-auditor--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED AUDITOR` syntax is used to query sharding rules used specified sharding auditor in specified logical database

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-auditor--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingTableRulesUsedAuditor::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'RULES' 'USED' 'AUDITOR' AuditortorName ('FROM' databaseName)?

AuditortorName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-auditor--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When databaseName is not specified, the default is the currently used DATABASE. If DATABASE is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-auditor--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Columns | Descriptions |
| --- | --- |
| type | Sharding rule type |
| name | Sharding rule name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-rules-used-auditor--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding table rules for the specified sharding auditor in spicified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED AUDITOR sharding_key_required_auditor FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED AUDITOR sharding_key_required_auditor FROM sharding_db;
+-------+---------+
| type  | name    |
+-------+---------+
| table | t_order |
+-------+---------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table rules for specified sharding auditor in the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED AUDITOR sharding_key_required_auditor;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE RULES USED AUDITOR sharding_key_required_auditor;
+-------+---------+
| type  | name    |
+-------+---------+
| table | t_order |
+-------+---------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/sharding/show-sharding-table-reference-rules.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--show-sharding-table-reference-rule"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--description"></a>

### Description

`SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE` syntax is used to query specified sharding table reference rule in the specified logical database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShardingBindingTableRules::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'TABLE' 'REFERENCE' ('RULE' ruleName | 'RULES') ('FROM' databaseName)?

ruleName ::=
    identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, No database selected will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Columns | Descriptions |
| --- | --- |
| name | Sharding table reference rule name |
| sharding\_table\_reference | sharding table reference |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sharding table reference rules for the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULES FROM sharding_db; +-------+--------------------------+ | name  | sharding_table_reference | +-------+--------------------------+ | ref_0 | t_a,t_b                  | | ref_1 | t_c,t_d                  | +-------+--------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query sharding table reference rules for the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULES; +-------+--------------------------+ | name  | sharding_table_reference | +-------+--------------------------+ | ref_0 | t_a,t_b                  | | ref_1 | t_c,t_d                  | +-------+--------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified sharding table reference rule for the specified logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE ref_0 FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE FROM sharding_db;
+-------+--------------------------+
| name  | sharding_table_reference |
+-------+--------------------------+
| ref_0 | t_a,t_b                  |
+-------+--------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified sharding table reference rule for the current logical database

```sql
SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE ref_0;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHARDING TABLE REFERENCE RULE ref_0;
+-------+--------------------------+
| name  | sharding_table_reference |
+-------+--------------------------+
| ref_0 | t_a,t_b                  |
+-------+--------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `TABLE`, `REFERENCE`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-show-sharding-table-reference-rules--related-links"></a>

### Related links

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/sharding/count-sharding-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

COUNT SHARDING RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--count-sharding-rule"></a>

# COUNT SHARDING RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT SHARDING RULE` syntax is used to query the number of sharding rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountShardingRule::=
  'COUNT' 'SHARDING' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of sharding rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT SHARDING RULE FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT SHARDING RULE FROM sharding_db; +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ | rule_name                | database       | count | +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ | sharding_table           | sharding_db    | 2     | | sharding_table_reference | sharding_db    | 2     | +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query the number of sharding rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT SHARDING RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT SHARDING RULE; +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ | rule_name                | database       | count | +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ | sharding_table           | sharding_db    | 2     | | sharding_table_reference | sharding_db    | 2     | +--------------------------+----------------+-------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `SHARDING`, `RULE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-sharding-count-sharding-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--show-broadcast-table-rule"></a>

# SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--description"></a>

## Description

The `SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULE` syntax is used to broadcast tables for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowBroadcastTableRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'BROADCAST' 'TABLE' 'RULES' ('FROM' databaseName)? 

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| broadcast\_table | Broadcast table name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query broadcast tables for specified database.

```sql
SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULES FROM sharding_db; +-----------------+ | broadcast_table | +-----------------+ | t_a             | | t_b             | | t_c             | +-----------------+ 3 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query broadcast table for current database.

```sql
SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW BROADCAST TABLE RULES; +-----------------+ | broadcast_table | +-----------------+ | t_a             | | t_b             | | t_c             | +-----------------+ 3 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `BROADCAST`, `TABLE`, `RULES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-show-broadcast-table-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--count-broadcast-rule"></a>

# COUNT BROADCAST RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT BROADCAST RULE` syntax is used to query the number of broadcast table rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountBroadcastRule::=
  'COUNT' 'BROADCAST' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of broadcast table rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT BROADCAST RULE FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT BROADCAST RULE FROM sharding_db;
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
| rule_name                | database       | count |
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
| broadcast_table          | sharding_db    | 0     |
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
1 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query the number of broadcast table rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT BROADCAST RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT BROADCAST RULE;
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
| rule_name                | database       | count |
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
| broadcast_table          | sharding_db    | 0     |
+--------------------------+----------------+-------+
1 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-broadcast-table-count-broadcast-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `BROADCAST`, `RULE`, `FROM`

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/single-table/show-single-table.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW SINGLE TABLE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--show-single-table"></a>

# SHOW SINGLE TABLE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SINGLE TABLE` syntax is used to query single tables for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowSingleTable::=
  'SHOW' 'SINGLE' ('TABLES' ('LIKES' likeLiteral)?|'TABLE' tableName) ('FROM' databaseName)?

tableName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| table\_name | Single table name |
| storage\_unit\_name | The storage unit name where the single table is located |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--example"></a>

### Example

- Query specified single table for specified database.

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLE t_user FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLE t_user FROM sharding_db;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_user     | ds_0              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified single table for current database.

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLE t_user;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLE t_user;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_user     | ds_0              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query single tables for specified database.

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLES FROM sharding_db;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_user     | ds_0              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query single tables for current database.

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLES;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_user     | ds_0              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query the single tables whose table name end with `order_5` for the specified logic database.

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLES LIKE '%order_5' FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLES LIKE '%order_5' FROM sharding_db;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_order_5  | ds_1              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.11 sec)
```

- Query the single tables whose table name end with `order_5` for the current logic database

```sql
SHOW SINGLE TABLES LIKE '%order_5';
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SINGLE TABLES LIKE '%order_5';
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_order_5  | ds_1              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.11 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`, `TABLES`, `LIKE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-single-table--related-links"></a>

# SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/single-table/show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--show-default-single-table-storage-unit"></a>

# SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT` syntax is used to query storage units for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowDefaultSingleTableStorageUnit::=
  'SHOW' 'DEFAULT' 'SINGLE' 'TABLE' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' ('FROM' databaseName)?
  
databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| storage\_unit\_name | Storage unit name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--example"></a>

### Example

- Query storage units for specified database.

```sql
SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT
```

```sql
sql> SHOW DEFAULT SINGLE TABLE STORAGE UNIT;
+-------------------+
| storage_unit_name |
+-------------------+
|  ds_0             |
+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `DEFAULT`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-deafult-single-table-storage-unit--related-links"></a>

### Related links

# COUNT SINGLE_TABLE RULE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/single-table/count-single-table.en.md "Edit this page")

COUNT SINGLE\_TABLE RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--count-single_table-rule"></a>

# COUNT SINGLE\_TABLE RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT SINGLE TABLE` syntax is used to query number of single table for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountSingleTable::=
  'COUNT' 'SINGLE' 'TABLE' ('FROM' databaseName)?
  
databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| database | The database name where the single table is located |
| count | The count of single table |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of single rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT SINGLE TABLE
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT SINGLE TABLE;
+----------+--------+
| database | count  |
+----------+--------+
| ds       | 2      |
+----------+--------+
1 row in set (0.02 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `SINGLE`, `TABLE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-count-single-table--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

SHOW UNLOADED SINGLE TABLES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--show-unloaded-single-tables"></a>

# SHOW UNLOADED SINGLE TABLES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--description)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW UNLOADED SINGLE TABLES` syntax is used to query unloaded single tables.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
showUnloadedSingleTables::=
  'SHOW' 'UNLOADED' 'SINGLE' 'TABLES'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| table\_name | Single table name |
| storage\_unit\_name | The storage unit name where the single table is located |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--example"></a>

### Example

- Query unloaded single tables.

```sql
SHOW UNLOADED SINGLE TABLES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW UNLOADED SINGLE TABLES;
+------------+-------------------+
| table_name | storage_unit_name |
+------------+-------------------+
| t_single   | ds_1              |
+------------+-------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-single-table-show-unloaded-single-tables--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

Readwrite-Splitting

This chapter describes the syntax of readwrite-splitting.

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/readwrite-splitting/show-readwrite-splitting-rule/ -->

<!-- page_index: 196 -->

# SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/readwrite-splitting/show-readwrite-splitting-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--show-readwrite_splitting-rule"></a>

# SHOW READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE` syntax is used to query specified readwrite-splitting rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowReadWriteSplittingRule::=
  'SHOW' 'READWRITE_SPLITTING' ('RULE' ruleName | 'RULES') ('FROM' databaseName)?

ruleName ::=
  identifier  

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Readwrite-splitting rule name |
| write\_data\_source\_name | Write data source name |
| read\_data\_source\_names | Read data source name list |
| transactional\_read\_query\_strategy | Routing strategy for read query within a transaction |
| load\_balancer\_type | Load balance algorithm type |
| load\_balancer\_props | Load balance algorithm parameter |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query readwrite-splitting rules for specified database.

```sql
SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name       | write_storage_unit_name | read_storage_unit_names | transactional_read_query_strategy | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| ms_group_0 | write_ds                | read_ds_0,read_ds_1     | DYNAMIC                           | random             |                     |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query readwrite-splitting rules for current database.

```sql
SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES;
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name       | write_storage_unit_name | read_storage_unit_names | transactional_read_query_strategy | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| ms_group_0 | write_ds                | read_ds_0,read_ds_1     | DYNAMIC                           | random             |                     |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query specified readwrite-splitting rule for specified database.

```sql
SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name       | write_storage_unit_name | read_storage_unit_names | transactional_read_query_strategy | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| ms_group_0 | write_ds                | read_ds_0,read_ds_1     | DYNAMIC                           | random             |                     |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query specified readwrite-splitting rule for current database.

```sql
SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0;
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name       | write_storage_unit_name | read_storage_unit_names | transactional_read_query_strategy | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| ms_group_0 | write_ds                | read_ds_0,read_ds_1     | DYNAMIC                           | random             |                     |
+------------+-------------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-show-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/readwrite-splitting/count-readwrite-splitting-rule/ -->

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/readwrite-splitting/count-readwrite-splitting-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

COUNT READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--count-readwrite_splitting-rule"></a>

# COUNT READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE` syntax is used to query the number of readwrite-splitting rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountReadwriteSplittingRule::=
  'COUNT' 'READWRITE_SPLITTING' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of readwrite-splitting rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE FROM readwrite_splitting_db;
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
| rule_name           | database                  | count |
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
| readwrite_splitting | readwrite_splitting_db    | 1     |
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.02 sec)
```

- Query the number of readwrite-splitting rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE;
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
| rule_name           | database                  | count |
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
| readwrite_splitting | readwrite_splitting_db    | 1     |
+---------------------+---------------------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-readwrite-splitting-count-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`, `FROM`

# SHOW ENCRYPT RULES

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/encrypt/show-encrypt-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW ENCRYPT RULES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--show-encrypt-rules"></a>

# SHOW ENCRYPT RULES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--syntax)
  - [Note](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--note)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW ENCRYPT RULES` syntax is used to query encryption rules for a specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowEncryptRule::=
  'SHOW' 'ENCRYPT' ('RULES' | 'TABLE' 'RULE' ruleName) ('FROM' databaseName)?

ruleName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--note"></a>

### Note

- When `databaseName` is not specified, then `DATABASE`is currently used as the default name. If `DATABASE` is not used, you will receive a `No database selected` prompt.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| table | Logical table name |
| logic\_column | Logical column name |
| cipher\_column | Ciphertext column name |
| assisted\_query\_column | Assisted query column name |
| like\_query\_column | Like query column name |
| encryptor\_type | Encryption algorithm type |
| encryptor\_props | Encryption algorithm parameter |
| assisted\_query\_type | Assisted query algorithm type |
| assisted\_query\_props | Assisted query algorithm parameter |
| like\_query\_type | Like query algorithm type |
| like\_query\_props | Like query algorithm parameter |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query encrypt rules for specified database.

```sql
SHOW ENCRYPT RULES FROM encrypt_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW ENCRYPT RULES FROM encrypt_db; +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ | table     | logic_column | cipher_column | assisted_query_column | like_query_column | encryptor_type | encryptor_props         | assisted_query_type | assisted_query_props | like_query_type | like_query_props | +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ | t_user    | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  | | t_encrypt | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  | +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query encrypt rules for current database.

```sql
SHOW ENCRYPT RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW ENCRYPT RULES; +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ | table     | logic_column | cipher_column | assisted_query_column | like_query_column | encryptor_type | encryptor_props         | assisted_query_type | assisted_query_props | like_query_type | like_query_props | +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ | t_user    | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  | | t_encrypt | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  | +-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified encrypt rule in specified database.

```sql
SHOW ENCRYPT TABLE RULE t_encrypt FROM encrypt_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW ENCRYPT TABLE RULE t_encrypt FROM encrypt_db;
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
| table     | logic_column | cipher_column | assisted_query_column | like_query_column | encryptor_type | encryptor_props         | assisted_query_type | assisted_query_props | like_query_type | like_query_props |
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
| t_encrypt | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  |
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query specified encrypt rule in current database.

```sql
SHOW ENCRYPT TABLE RULE t_encrypt;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW ENCRYPT TABLE RULE t_encrypt;
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
| table     | logic_column | cipher_column | assisted_query_column | like_query_column | encryptor_type | encryptor_props         | assisted_query_type | assisted_query_props | like_query_type | like_query_props |
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
| t_encrypt | pwd          | pwd_cipher    |                       |                   | AES            | aes-key-value=123456abc, digest-algorithm-name=SHA-1 |                     |                      |                 |                  |
+-----------+--------------+---------------+-----------------------+-------------------+----------------+-------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-----------------+------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `ENCRYPT`, `TABLE`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-show-encrypt-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

COUNT ENCRYPT RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--count-encrypt-rule"></a>

# COUNT ENCRYPT RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT ENCRYPT RULE` syntax is used to query the number of encrypt rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountEncryptRule::=
  'COUNT' 'ENCRYPT' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of encrypt rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT ENCRYPT RULE FROM encrypt_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT ENCRYPT RULE FROM encrypt_db;
+-----------+---------------+-------+
| rule_name | database      | count |
+-----------+---------------+-------+
| encrypt   | encrypt_db    | 2     |
+-----------+---------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query the number of encrypt rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT ENCRYPT RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT ENCRYPT RULE;
+-----------+---------------+-------+
| rule_name | database      | count |
+-----------+---------------+-------+
| encrypt   | encrypt_db    | 2     |
+-----------+---------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `ENCRYPT`, `RULE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-encrypt-count-encrypt-rule--related-links"></a>
