<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--show-mask-rules"></a>

# SHOW MASK RULES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--example)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MASK RULES` syntax is used to query mask rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowMaskRule::=
  'SHOW' 'MASK' ('RULES' | 'RULE' ruleName) ('FROM' databaseName)?

ruleName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| table | Table name |
| column | Column name |
| algorithm\_type | Mask algorithm type |
| algorithm\_props | Mask algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query mask rules for specified database

```sql
SHOW MASK RULES FROM mask_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MASK RULES FROM mask_db; +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | table   | column   | algorithm_type   | algorithm_props                | +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | t_mask  | phoneNum | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | | t_mask  | address  | MD5              |                                | | t_order | order_id | MD5              |                                | | t_user  | user_id  | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ 4 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query mask rules for current database

```sql
SHOW MASK RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MASK RULES; +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | table   | column   | algorithm_type   | algorithm_props                | +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | t_mask  | phoneNum | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | | t_mask  | address  | MD5              |                                | | t_order | order_id | MD5              |                                | | t_user  | user_id  | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | +---------+----------+------------------+--------------------------------+ 4 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query specified mask rule for specified database

```sql
SHOW MASK RULE t_mask FROM mask_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MASK RULE t_mask FROM mask_db; +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | table  | logic_column | mask_algorithm   | props                          | +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | t_mask | phoneNum     | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | | t_mask | address      | MD5              |                                | +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified mask rule for current database

```sql
SHOW MASK RULE t_mask;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MASK RULE t_mask; +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | table  | logic_column | mask_algorithm   | props                          | +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ | t_mask | phoneNum     | MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y | to-y=2,replace-char=*,from-x=1 | | t_mask | address      | MD5              |                                | +--------+--------------+------------------+--------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MASK`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-show-mask-rule--related-links"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT MASK RULE` syntax is used to query the number of mask rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountMaskRule::=
  'COUNT' 'MASK' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of mask rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT MASK RULE FROM mask_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT MASK RULE FROM mask_db;
+-----------+----------+-------+
| rule_name | database | count |
+-----------+----------+-------+
| mask      | mask_db  | 3     |
+-----------+----------+-------+
1 row in set (0.50 sec)
```

- Query the number of mask rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT MASK RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT MASK RULE;
+-----------+----------+-------+
| rule_name | database | count |
+-----------+----------+-------+
| mask      | mask_db  | 3     |
+-----------+----------+-------+
1 row in set (0.50 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `MASK`, `RULE`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-mask-count-mask-rule--related-links"></a>

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rql/rule-query/shadow/show-shadow-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW SHADOW RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--show-shadow-rule"></a>

# SHOW SHADOW RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHADOW RULE` syntax is used to query shadow rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShadowRule::=
  'SHOW' 'SHADOW' ('RULES' | 'RULE' shadowRuleName) ('FROM' databaseName)?

shadowRuleName ::=
  identifier
  
databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | Shadow rule name |
| source\_name | Data source name |
| shadow\_name | Shadow data source name |
| shadow\_table | Shadow table |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query specified shadow rule in specified database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW RULE shadow_rule FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW RULE shadow_rule FROM shadow_db;
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| rule_name   | source_name | shadow_name | shadow_table         |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| shadow_rule | ds_0        | ds_1        | t_order_item,t_order |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query specified shadow rule in current database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW RULE shadow_rule;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW RULE shadow_rule;
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| rule_name   | source_name | shadow_name | shadow_table         |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| shadow_rule | ds_0        | ds_1        | t_order_item,t_order |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query shadow rules for specified database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW RULES FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW RULES FROM shadow_db;
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| rule_name   | source_name | shadow_name | shadow_table         |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| shadow_rule | ds_0        | ds_1        | t_order_item,t_order |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query shadow rules for current database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW RULES;
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| rule_name   | source_name | shadow_name | shadow_table         |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
| shadow_rule | ds_0        | ds_1        | t_order_item,t_order |
+-------------+-------------+-------------+----------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHADOW`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--show-shadow-table-rules"></a>

# SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES` syntax is used to query shadow table rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShadowTableRule::=
  'SHOW' 'SHADOW' 'TABLE' 'RULES' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| shadow\_table | Shadow table |
| shadow\_algorithm\_name | Shadow algorithm name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query shadow table rules for specified database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES FROM shadow_db; +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ | shadow_table | shadow_algorithm_name                                 | +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ | t_order_item | shadow_rule_t_order_item_value_match                  | | t_order      | sql_hint_algorithm,shadow_rule_t_order_regex_match | +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query shadow table rules for current database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW TABLE RULES; +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ | shadow_table | shadow_algorithm_name                                 | +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ | t_order_item | shadow_rule_t_order_item_value_match                  | | t_order      | sql_hint_algorithm,shadow_rule_t_order_regex_match | +--------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-table-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHADOW`, `TABLE`, `RULES`, `FROM`

SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--show-shadow-algorithms"></a>

# SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--supplement)

  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS` syntax is used to query shadow algorithms for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowShadowAlgorithm::=
  'SHOW' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHMS' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| shadow\_algorithm\_name | Shadow algorithm name |
| type | Shadow algorithm type |
| props | Shadow algorithm properties |
| is\_default | Default |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Query shadow algorithms for specified database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS FROM shadow_db;
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
| shadow_algorithm_name   | type        | props                                   | is_default |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
| user_id_match_algorithm | VALUE_MATCH | column=user_id,operation=insert,value=1 | false      |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query shadow algorithms for current database.

```sql
SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SHADOW ALGORITHMS;
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
| shadow_algorithm_name   | type        | props                                   | is_default |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
| user_id_match_algorithm | VALUE_MATCH | column=user_id,operation=insert,value=1 | false      |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHADOW`, `ALGORITHMS`, `FROM`

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--description)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM` syntax is used to query default shadow algorithm for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowDefaultShadowAlgorithm::=
  'SHOW' 'DEFAULT' 'SHADOW' 'ALGORITHM' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| shadow\_algorithm\_name | Shadow algorithm name |
| type | Shadow algorithm type |
| props | Shadow algorithm properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Query default shadow algorithm for specified database.

```sql
SHOW DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM FROM shadow_db;
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
| shadow_algorithm_name   | type        | props                                   |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
| user_id_match_algorithm | VALUE_MATCH | column=user_id,operation=insert,value=1 |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query default shadow algorithm for current database.

```sql
SHOW DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DEFAULT SHADOW ALGORITHM;
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
| shadow_algorithm_name   | type        | props                                   |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
| user_id_match_algorithm | VALUE_MATCH | column=user_id,operation=insert,value=1 |
+-------------------------+-------------+-----------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-show-default-shadow-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `DEFAULT`,`SHADOW`, `ALGORITHM`, `FROM`

COUNT SHADOW RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--count-shadow-rule"></a>

# COUNT SHADOW RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--supplement)
  - [Return value description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `COUNT SHADOW RULE` syntax is used to query the number of shadow rules for specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
CountShadowRule::=
  'COUNT' 'SHADOW' 'RULE' ('FROM' databaseName)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return value description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| rule\_name | rule type |
| database | the database to which the rule belongs |
| count | the number of the rule |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the number of shadow rules for specified database.

```sql
COUNT SHADOW RULE FROM shadow_db;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT SHADOW RULE FROM shadow_db;
+-----------+--------------+-------+
| rule_name | database     | count |
+-----------+--------------+-------+
| shadow    | shadow_db    | 1     |
+-----------+--------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query the number of shadow rules for current database.

```sql
COUNT SHADOW RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> COUNT SHADOW RULE;
+-----------+--------------+-------+
| rule_name | database     | count |
+-----------+--------------+-------+
| shadow    | shadow_db    | 1     |
+-----------+--------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rql-rule-query-shadow-count-shadow-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`COUNT`, `SHADOW`, `RULE`, `FROM`

# RAL Syntax :: ShardingSphere

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/_index.en.md "Edit this page")

RAL Syntax

RAL (Resource & Rule Administration Language) responsible for the added-on feature of transaction type switch, scaling and so on.

SHOW AUTHORITY RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--show-authority-rule"></a>

# SHOW AUTHORITY RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW AUTHORITY RULE` syntax is used to query authority rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowAuthorityRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'AUTHORITY' 'RULE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| users | users |
| provider | privilege provider type |
| props | privilege properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query authority rule configuration

```sql
SHOW AUTHORITY RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW AUTHORITY RULE;
+--------------------+---------------+-------+
| users              | provider      | props |
+--------------------+---------------+-------+
| root@%; sharding@% | ALL_PERMITTED |       |
+--------------------+---------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.07 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `AUTHORITY`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-authority-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--description)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW TRANSACTION RULE` syntax is used to query transaction rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowTransactionRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'TRANSACTION' 'RULE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| users | users |
| provider | privilege provider type |
| props | privilege properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query transaction rule configuration

```sql
SHOW TRANSACTION RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TRANSACTION RULE;
+--------------+---------------+-------+
| default_type | provider_type | props |
+--------------+---------------+-------+
| LOCAL        |               |       |
+--------------+---------------+-------+
1 row in set (0.05 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-transaction-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `TRANSACTION`, `RULE`

ALTER TRANSACTION RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--alter-transaction-rule"></a>

# ALTER TRANSACTION RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER TRANSACTION RULE` syntax is used to alter transaction rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterTransactionRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'TRANSACTION' 'RULE' '(' 'DEFAULT' '=' defaultTransactionType ',' 'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' transactionManager ',' propertiesDefinition ')' ')'

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' key '=' value (',' key '=' value)* ')'

defaultTransactionType ::=
  string

transactionManager ::=
  string

key ::=
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `defaultTransactionType` support `LOCAL`, `XA`, `BASE`
- `transactionManager` support `Atomikos` and `Narayana`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter transaction rule

```sql
ALTER TRANSACTION RULE(
  DEFAULT="XA", TYPE(NAME="Narayana")
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `TRANSACTION`, `RULE`, `DEFAULT`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-transaction-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--syntax)

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SQL_PARSER RULE` syntax is used to query sql parser rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowSqlParserRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'SQL_PARSER' 'RULE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| parse\_tree\_cache | parse tree cache configuration |
| sql\_statement\_cache | SQL statement cache configuration |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sql parser rule configuration

```sql
SHOW SQL_PARSER RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW SQL_PARSER RULE;
+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| parse_tree_cache                        | sql_statement_cache                       |
+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| initialCapacity: 128, maximumSize: 1024 | initialCapacity: 2000, maximumSize: 65535 |
+-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.05 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-parser-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SQL_PARSER`, `RULE`

Alter SQL\_PARSER Rule

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--alter-sql_parser-rule"></a>

# Alter SQL\_PARSER Rule

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--syntax)
  - [Note](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--note)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER SQL_PARSER RULE` syntax is used to alter the SQL parser rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterSqlParserRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'SQL_PARSER' 'RULE' '(' sqlParserRuleDefinition ')'

sqlParserRuleDefinition ::=
  parseTreeCacheDefinition? (',' sqlStatementCacheDefinition)?

parseTreeCacheDefinition ::=
  'PARSE_TREE_CACHE' '(' cacheOption ')'

sqlStatementCacheDefinition ::=
  'SQL_STATEMENT_CACHE' '(' cacheOption ')'

cacheOption ::=
  ('INITIAL_CAPACITY' '=' initialCapacity)? (','? 'MAXIMUM_SIZE' '=' maximumSize)?

initialCapacity ::=
  int

maximumSize ::=
  int
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--note"></a>

### Note

- `PARSE_TREE_CACHE`: local cache configuration of the syntax tree.
- `SQL_STATEMENT_CACHE`: the local cache of SQL statement.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter SQL parser rule

```sql
ALTER SQL_PARSER RULE (
  PARSE_TREE_CACHE(INITIAL_CAPACITY=128, MAXIMUM_SIZE=1024), 
  SQL_STATEMENT_CACHE(INITIAL_CAPACITY=2000, MAXIMUM_SIZE=65535)
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `SQL_PARSER`, `RULE`, `PARSE_TREE_CACHE`, `INITIAL_CAPACITY`, `MAXIMUM_SIZE`, `SQL_STATEMENT_CACHE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-parser-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# ALTER SQL_TRANSLATOR RULE

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/global-rule/alter-sql-translator-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

ALTER SQL\_TRANSLATOR RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--alter-sql_translator-rule"></a>

# ALTER SQL\_TRANSLATOR RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--syntax)
  - [Note](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--note)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER SQL_TRANSLATOR RULE` syntax is used to alter the SQL translator rule configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterSQLTranslatorRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'SQL_TRANSLATOR' 'RULE' '(' sqlTranslatorRuleDefinition ')'

sqlTranslatorRuleDefinition ::=
  algorithmDefinition (',' useOriginalSQLWhenTranslatingFailedClause)?

algorithmDefinition ::=
  'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' translatorType (',' 'PROPERTIES' '(' propertiesDefinition ')' )? ')'

useOriginalSQLWhenTranslatingFailedClause ::=
  'USE_ORIGINAL_SQL_WHEN_TRANSLATING_FAILED' '=' booleanLiteral

translatorType ::=
  identifier

propertiesDefinition ::=
  property (',' property)*

property ::=
  key '=' value

booleanLiteral ::=
  'TRUE' | 'FALSE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--note"></a>

### Note

- `TYPE` declares the SQL translator implementation and its `PROPERTIES`.
- `USE_ORIGINAL_SQL_WHEN_TRANSLATING_FAILED` controls whether the original SQL should be used when translation fails.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter SQL translator rule with fallback on translation failure

```sql
ALTER SQL_TRANSLATOR RULE (
  TYPE(NAME='NATIVE', PROPERTIES('key'='value')),
  USE_ORIGINAL_SQL_WHEN_TRANSLATING_FAILED=TRUE
);
```

- Alter SQL translator rule without specifying fallback

```sql
ALTER SQL_TRANSLATOR RULE (
  TYPE(NAME='NATIVE', PROPERTIES('key'='value'))
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `SQL_TRANSLATOR`, `RULE`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`, `USE_ORIGINAL_SQL_WHEN_TRANSLATING_FAILED`, `TRUE`, `FALSE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-translator-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/global-rule/show-traffic-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW TRAFFIC RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--show-traffic-rule"></a>

# SHOW TRAFFIC RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW TRAFFIC RULE` syntax is used to query specified dual routing rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowTrafficRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'TRAFFIC' ('RULES' | 'RULE' ruleName)?

ruleName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `ruleName` not specified, the default is show all traffic rules

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | traffic rule name |
| labels | compute node labels |
| algorithm\_type | traffic algorithm type |
| algorithm\_props | traffic algorithn properties |
| load\_balancer\_type | load balancer type |
| load\_balancer\_props | load balancer properties |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query specified traffic rule

```sql
SHOW TRAFFIC RULE sql_match_traffic;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TRAFFIC RULE sql_match_traffic;
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name              | labels | algorithm_type | algorithm_props                                                                | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| sql_match_traffic | OLTP   | SQL_MATCH      | sql=SELECT * FROM t_order WHERE order_id = 1; UPDATE t_order SET order_id = 5; | RANDOM             |                     |
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query all traffic rules

```sql
SHOW TRAFFIC RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TRAFFIC RULES;
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| name              | labels | algorithm_type | algorithm_props                                                                | load_balancer_type | load_balancer_props |
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
| sql_match_traffic | OLTP   | SQL_MATCH      | sql=SELECT * FROM t_order WHERE order_id = 1; UPDATE t_order SET order_id = 5; | RANDOM             |                     |
+-------------------+--------+----------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------+---------------------+
1 row in set (0.04 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `TRAFFIC`, `RULE`, `RULES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-traffic-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<!-- page_index: 220 -->

# ALTER TRAFFIC RULE

Haitian

Deep

Dark

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/global-rule/alter-traffic-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

ALTER TRAFFIC RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--alter-traffic-rule"></a>

# ALTER TRAFFIC RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER TRAFFIC RULE` syntax is used to alter dual routing rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterTrafficRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'TRAFFIC' 'RULE'  '(' 'LABELS' '(' lableName ')' ',' trafficAlgorithmDefinition ',' loadBalancerDefinition ')'

lableName ::=
  identifier

trafficAlgorithmDefinition ::=
  'TRAFFIC_ALGORITHM' '(' 'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' trafficAlgorithmTypeName (',' propertiesDefinition)? ')' ')'

loadBalancerDefinition ::=
  'LOAD_BALANCER' '(' 'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' loadBalancerName (',' propertiesDefinition)? ')' ')'

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' key '=' value (',' key '=' value)* ')'

trafficAlgorithmTypeName ::=
  string

loadBalancerTypeName ::=
  string

key ::= 
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `TRAFFIC_ALGORITHM` support `SQL_MATCH` and `SQL_HINT` two types;
- `LOAD_BALANCER` support `RANDOM` and `ROUND_ROBIN` two types.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter dual routing rule

```sql
 TRAFFIC RULE sql_match_traffic ( 
  LABELS (OLTP),
  TRAFFIC_ALGORITHM(TYPE(NAME="SQL_MATCH",PROPERTIES("sql" = "SELECT * FROM t_order WHERE order_id = 1; UPDATE t_order SET order_id = 5;"))),
  LOAD_BALANCER(TYPE(NAME="RANDOM")));
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `TRAFFIC`, `RULE`, `LABELS`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`, `TRAFFIC_ALGORITHM`, `LOAD_BALANCER`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-traffic-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/global-rule/show-sql-federation-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW SQL\_FEDERATION RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--show-sql_federation-rule"></a>

# SHOW SQL\_FEDERATION RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SQL_FEDERATION RULE` syntax is used to query the federated query configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowSQLFederationRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'SQL_FEDERATION' 'RULE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| sql\_federation\_enabled | SQL federation enabled configuration |
| all\_query\_use\_sql\_federation | all query use SQL federation configuration |
| execution\_plan\_cache | execution plan cache configuration |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query sql federation rule configuration

```sql
SHOW SQL_FEDERATION RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> show sql_federation rule;
+------------------------+------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| sql_federation_enabled | all_query_use_sql_federation | execution_plan_cache                      |
+------------------------+------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| true                   | false                        | initialCapacity: 2000, maximumSize: 65535 |
+------------------------+------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.31 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`、`SQL_FEDERATION`、`RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-show-sql-federation-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# ALTER SQL_FEDERATION RULE

ALTER SQL\_FEDERATION RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--alter-sql_federation-rule"></a>

# ALTER SQL\_FEDERATION RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--syntax)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER SQL_FEDERATION RULE` syntax is used to modify the federated query configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterSQLFederationRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'SQL_FEDERATION' 'RULE' sqlFederationRuleDefinition

sqlFederationRuleDefinition ::=
  '(' sqlFederationEnabled? (','? allQueryUseSQLFederation)? (','? executionPlanCache)? ')'

sqlFederationEnabled ::=
  'SQL_FEDERATION_ENABLED' '=' boolean_

allQueryUseSQLFederation ::=
  'ALL_QUERY_USE_SQL_FEDERATION' '=' boolean_

executionPlanCache ::=
  'EXECUTION_PLAN_CACHE' '(' cacheOption ')'

cacheOption ::=
  ('INITIAL_CAPACITY' '=' initialCapacity)? (',' 'MAXIMUM_SIZE' '=' maximumSize)?

initialCapacity ::=
  int

maximumSize ::=
  int

boolean_ ::=
  TRUE | FALSE
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Alter SQL Federation rule

```sql
ALTER SQL_FEDERATION RULE (
  SQL_FEDERATION_ENABLED=TRUE,
  ALL_QUERY_USE_SQL_FEDERATION=TRUE,
  EXECUTION_PLAN_CACHE(INITIAL_CAPACITY=1024, MAXIMUM_SIZE=65535)
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`、`SQL_FEDERATION`、`RULE`、`SQL_FEDERATION_ENABLED`、`ALL_QUERY_USE_SQL_FEDERATION`、`EXECUTION_PLAN_CACHE`、`INITIAL_CAPACITY`、`MAXIMUM_SIZE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-global-rule-alter-sql-federation-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ENABLE/DISABLE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/circuit-breaker/alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable.en.md "Edit this page")

ALTER READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE ENABLE/DISABLE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--alter-readwrite_splitting-rule-enable-disable"></a>

# ALTER READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE ENABLE/DISABLE

- [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--description"></a>

## Description

The `ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ENABLE/DISABLE` syntax is used enable/disable a specified read source for specified readwrite-splitting rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterReadwriteSplittingRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'READWRITE_SPLITTING' 'RULE' groupName ('ENABLE' | 'DISABLE') storageUnitName 'FROM' databaseName

groupName ::=
  identifier

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--example"></a>

### Example

- Disable a specified read source for specified readwrite-splitting rule in specified database

```sql
ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 DISABLE read_ds_0 FROM sharding_db;
```

- Enable a specified read source for specified readwrite-splitting rule in specified database

```sql
ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 ENABLE read_ds_0 FROM sharding_db;
```

- Disable a specified read source for specified readwrite-splitting rule in current database

```sql
ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 DISABLE read_ds_0;
```

- Enable a specified read source for specified readwrite-splitting rule in current database

```sql
ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_1 ENABLE read_ds_0;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`, `ENABLE`, `DISABLE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-alter-readwrite-splitting-rule-enable-disable--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/circuit-breaker/show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule/ -->

# SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE

Eye care

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/circuit-breaker/show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--show-status-from-readwrite_splitting-rule"></a>

# SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE\_SPLITTING RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE` syntax is used to query readwrite-splitting storage unit status for specified readwrite-splitting rule in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowStatusFromReadwriteSplittingRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'STATUS' 'FROM' 'READWRITE_SPLITTING' ('RULES' | 'RULE' groupName) ('FROM' databaseName)?

groupName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | readwrite-splitting rule name |
| storage\_unit | storage unit name |
| status | storage unit status |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query readwrite-splitting storage unit status for specified readwrite-splitting rule in specified database.

```sql
SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0 FROM sharding_db;
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| name        | storage_unit | status   |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| ms_group_0  | ds_0         | disabled |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
1 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query all readwrite-splitting storage unit from specified database

```sql
SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES FROM sharding_db;
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| name        | storage_unit | status   |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| ms_group_0  | ds_0         | disabled |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
1 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query readwrite-splitting storage unit status for specified readwrite-splitting rule in current database

```sql
SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE ms_group_0;
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| name        | storage_unit | status   |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| ms_group_0  | ds_0         | disabled |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
1 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query all readwrite-splitting storage unit from current database

```sql
mysql> SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW STATUS FROM READWRITE_SPLITTING RULES;
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| name        | storage_unit | status   |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
| ms_group_0  | ds_0         | disabled |
+-------------+--------------+----------+
1 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-status-from-readwrite-splitting-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `STATUS`, `FROM`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`, `RULES`

SHOW COMPUTE NODES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--show-compute-nodes"></a>

# SHOW COMPUTE NODES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--example)
  - [Dedicated Terminology](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--dedicated-terminology)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW COMPUTE NODES` syntax is used to query compute nodes information.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowComputeNodes ::=
  'SHOW' 'COMPUTE' 'NODES'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| instance\_id | instance id |
| instance\_type | instance type |
| host | host |
| port | port |
| status | status |
| mode\_type | mode type |
| worker\_id | worker id |
| labels | labels |
| version | version |
| database\_name | database name (Only valid for JDBC) |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
mysql> SHOW COMPUTE NODES;
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+--------------+
| instance_id                          | instance_type | host       | port | status | mode_type  | worker_id | labels | version  | database_name |
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+--------------+
| 3e84d33e-cb97-42f2-b6ce-f78fea0ded89 | PROXY         | 127.0.0.1  | 3307 | OK     | Cluster    | -1        |        | 5.4.2    | logic_db      |
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+--------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-circuit-breaker-show-compute-nodes--dedicated-terminology"></a>

### Dedicated Terminology

`SHOW`, `COMPUTE`, `NODES`

SHOW MIGRATION RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--show-migration-rule"></a>

# SHOW MIGRATION RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MIGRATION RULE` syntax is used to query migration rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowMigrationRule ::=
  'SHOW' 'MIGRATION' 'RULE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| read | Data reading configuration |
| write | Data writing configuration |
| stream\_channel | Data channel |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--example"></a>

### Example

- Query migration rule

```sql
SHOW MIGRATION RULE;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MIGRATION RULE;
+--------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
| read                                                         | write                                | stream_channel                                        |
+--------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
| {"workerThread":20,"batchSize":1000,"shardingSize":10000000} | {"workerThread":20,"batchSize":1000} | {"type":"MEMORY","props":{"block-queue-size":"2000"}} |
+--------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MIGRATION`, `RULE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-rule--related-links"></a>

# ALTER MIGRATION RULE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/alter-migration-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

ALTER MIGRATION RULE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--alter-migration-rule"></a>

# ALTER MIGRATION RULE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--syntax)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--description"></a>

### Description

The `ALTER MIGRATION RULE` syntax is used to alter migration rule.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
AlterMigrationRule ::=
  'ALTER' 'MIGRATION' 'RULE' ('(' (readConfiguration ',')?  (writeConfiguration  ',')? (dataChannel)? ')')?

readConfiguration ::=
  'READ' '(' ('WORKER_THREAD' '=' workerThreadPoolSize ',')? ('BATCH_SIZE' '=' batchSize ',')? ('SHARDING_SIZE' '=' shardingSize ',')? (rateLimiter)? ')'

writeConfiguration ::=
  'WRITE' '(' ('WORKER_THREAD' '=' workerThreadPoolSize ',')? ('BATCH_SIZE' '=' batchSize ',')? ('SHARDING_SIZE' '=' shardingSize ',')? (rateLimiter)? ')'

dataChannel ::=
  'STREAM_CHANNEL' '(' 'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' algorithmName ',' propertiesDefinition ')' ')'

workerThreadPoolSize ::=
  int

batchSize ::=
  int

shardingSize ::=
  int

rateLimiter ::=
  'RATE_LIMITER' '(' 'TYPE' '(' 'NAME' '=' algorithmName ',' propertiesDefinition ')' ')'

algorithmName ::=
  string

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' key '=' value (',' key '=' value)* ')'

key ::=
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
ALTER MIGRATION RULE (
  READ( WORKER_THREAD=20, BATCH_SIZE=1000, SHARDING_SIZE=10000000, RATE_LIMITER (TYPE(NAME='QPS',PROPERTIES('qps'='500')))), 
  WRITE( WORKER_THREAD=20, BATCH_SIZE=1000, RATE_LIMITER (TYPE(NAME='TPS',PROPERTIES('tps'='2000')))), 
  STREAM_CHANNEL ( TYPE(NAME='MEMORY',PROPERTIES('block-queue-size'='2000')))
  );
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`ALTER`, `MIGRATION`, `RULE`, `READ`, `WRITE`, `WORKER_THREAD`, `BATCH_SIZE`, `SHARDING_SIZE`, `STREAM_CHANNEL`, `TYPE`, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-alter-migration-rule--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/register-migration-source-storage-unit/ -->

<!-- page_index: 233 -->

# REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/register-migration-source-storage-unit.en.md "Edit this page")

REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--register-migration-source-storage-unit"></a>

# REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--description"></a>

### Description

The `REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT` syntax is used to register migration source storage unit for the currently connection.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
RegisterStorageUnit ::=
  'REGISTER' 'MIGRATION' 'SOURCE' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' storageUnitDefinition (',' storageUnitDefinition)*

storageUnitDefinition ::=
  StorageUnitName '('  'URL' '=' url ',' 'USER' '=' user (',' 'PASSWORD' '=' password)? (',' propertiesDefinition)?')'

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier

url ::=
  string

user ::=
  string

password ::=
  string

propertiesDefinition ::=
  'PROPERTIES' '(' ( key  '=' value ) ( ',' key  '=' value )* ')'

key ::=
  string

value ::=
  literal
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- Confirm that the registered migration source storage unit can be connected normally, otherwise it will not be added successfully;
- `storageUnitName` is case-sensitive;
- `storageUnitName` needs to be unique within the current connection;
- `storageUnitName` name only allows letters, numbers and `_`, and must start with a letter;
- `poolProperty` is used to customize connection pool parameters, `key` must be the same as the connection pool
  parameter name, `value` supports int and String types;
- When `password` contains special characters, it is recommended to use the string form; For example, the string form
  of `password@123` is `"password@123"`.
- The data migration source storage unit currently only supports registration using `URL`, and temporarily does not support using `HOST` and `PORT`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--example"></a>

### Example

- Register migration source storage unit

```sql
REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    URL="jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/migration_ds_0?serverTimezone=UTC&useSSL=false",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

- Register migration source storage unit and set connection pool parameters

```sql
REGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    URL="jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/migration_ds_0?serverTimezone=UTC&useSSL=false",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root",
    PROPERTIES("minPoolSize"="1","maxPoolSize"="20","idleTimeout"="60000")
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`REGISTER`, `MIGRATION`, `SOURCE`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`, `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `PROPERTIES`, `URL`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-register-migration-source-storage-unit--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

Haitian
