UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--unregister-migration-source-storage-unit"></a>

# UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--description"></a>

### Description

The `UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT` syntax is used to unregister migration source storage unit from the current connection

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
UnregisterMigrationSourceStorageUnit ::=
  'UNREGISTER' 'MIGRATION' 'SOURCE' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' storageUnitName  (',' storageUnitName)* 

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT` will only unregister storage unit in Proxy, the real data source corresponding to the storage unit will not be
  dropped;

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--example"></a>

### Example

- Drop a migration source storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT ds_0;
```

- Drop multiple migration source storage units

```sql
UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT ds_1, ds_2;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`UNREGISTER`、`MIGRATION`、`SOURCE`、`STORAGE`、`UNIT`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-unregister-storage-unit--related-links"></a>

### Related links

SHOW MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNITS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--show-migration-source-storage-units"></a>

# SHOW MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNITS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNITS` syntax is used to query the registered migration source storage units

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowStorageUnit ::=
  'SHOW' 'MIGRATION' 'SOURCE' 'STORAGE' 'UNITS'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| name | Storage unit name |
| type | Storage unit type |
| host | Storage unit host |
| port | Storage unit port |
| db | Database name |
| attribute | Storage unit attribute |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--example"></a>

### Example

- Query registered migration source storage units

```sql
SHOW MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNITS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNITS;
+------+-------+-----------+------+----------------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+------------------+
| name | type  | host      | port | db             | connection_timeout_milliseconds | idle_timeout_milliseconds | max_lifetime_milliseconds | max_pool_size | min_pool_size | read_only | other_attributes |
+------+-------+-----------+------+----------------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+------------------+
| ds_1 | MySQL | 127.0.0.1 | 3306 | migration_ds_0 |                                 |                           |                           |               |               |           |                  |
+------+-------+-----------+------+----------------+---------------------------------+---------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+---------------+-----------+------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MIGRATION`, `SOURCE`, `STORAGE`, `UNITS`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-source-storage-units--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/migrate-table-into/ -->

<!-- page_index: 236 -->

# MIGRATE TABLE INTO

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/migrate-table-into.en.md "Edit this page")

MIGRATE TABLE INTO

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--migrate-table-into"></a>

# MIGRATE TABLE INTO

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--description"></a>

### Description

`MIGRATE TABLE INTO` syntax is used to migration table from source to target

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
MigrateTableInto ::=
  'MIGRATE' 'TABLE' migrationSource '.' tableName 'INTO' (databaseName '.')? tableName

migrationSource ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier

tableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--example"></a>

### Example

- Migrate table from source to current database

```sql
MIGRATE TABLE ds_0.t_order INTO t_order;
```

- Migrate table from source to specified database

```sql
UNREGISTER MIGRATION SOURCE STORAGE UNIT ds_1, ds_2;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`MIGRATE`, `TABLE`, `INTO`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-migrate-table-into--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# SHOW MIGRATION LIST

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/show-migration-list.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW MIGRATION LIST

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--show-migration-list"></a>

# SHOW MIGRATION LIST

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--syntax)
  - [Return Values Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--return-values-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MIGRATION LIST` syntax is used to query migration job list.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowMigrationList ::=
  'SHOW' 'MIGRATION' 'LIST'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--return-values-description"></a>

### Return Values Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | migration job id |
| tables | migration tables |
| job\_item\_count | migration job sharding number |
| active | migration job states |
| create\_time | migration job create time |
| stop\_time | migration job stop time |
| job\_sharding\_nodes | migration job sharding nodes |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--example"></a>

### Example

- Query migration job list

```sql
SHOW MIGRATION LIST;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MIGRATION LIST;
+--------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------+---------------------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+
| id                                         | tables              | active | create_time         | stop_time | job_item_count | job_sharding_nodes |
+--------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------+---------------------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+
| j0102p00001d029afca1fd960d567fed6cddc9b4a2 | source_ds.t_order   | true   | 2022-10-31 18:18:24 |           | 1              | 10.7.5.76@-@27808  |
+--------------------------------------------+---------------------+--------+---------------------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+
4 rows in set (0.06 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MIGRATION`, `LIST`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-list--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/show-migration-status/ -->

<!-- page_index: 238 -->

# SHOW MIGRATION STATUS

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/migration/show-migration-status.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW MIGRATION STATUS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--show-migration-status"></a>

# SHOW MIGRATION STATUS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--supplement)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MIGRATION STATUS` syntax is used to query migration job status for specified migration job.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowMigrationStatus ::=
  'SHOW' 'MIGRATION' 'STATUS' migrationJobId 

migrationJobId ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `migrationJobId` needs to be obtained through `SHOW MIGRATION LIST` syntax query

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| item | migration job sharding serial number |
| data source | migration source |
| status | migration job status |
| processed\_records\_count | number of processed rows |
| inventory\_finished\_percentage | finished percentage of migration job |
| incremental\_idle\_seconds | incremental idle time |
| error\_message | error message |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--example"></a>

### Example

- Query migration job status

```sql
SHOW MIGRATION STATUS 'j010180026753ef0e25d3932d94d1673ba551';
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MIGRATION STATUS 'j010180026753ef0e25d3932d94d1673ba551';
+------+-------------+--------------------------+--------+-------------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------+---------------+
| item | data_source | status                   | active | processed_records_count | inventory_finished_percentage | incremental_idle_seconds | error_message |
+------+-------------+--------------------------+--------+-------------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------+---------------+
| 0    | ds_1        | EXECUTE_INCREMENTAL_TASK | true   | 6                       | 100                           | 25                       |               |
+------+-------------+--------------------------+--------+-------------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------------+---------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MIGRATION`, `STATUS`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-status--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

SHOW MIGRATION CHECK ALGORITHM

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--show-migration-check-algorithm"></a>

# SHOW MIGRATION CHECK ALGORITHM

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW MIGRATION RULE` syntax is used to query migration check algorithm.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowMigrationCheckAlgorithm ::=
  'SHOW' 'MIGRATION' 'CHECK' 'ALGORITHMS'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| type | migration check algorithm type |
| supported\_database\_types | supported database type |
| description | Description of migration check algorithm |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--example"></a>

### Example

- Query migration check algorithm

```sql
SHOW MIGRATION CHECK ALGORITHMS;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW MIGRATION CHECK ALGORITHMS; +-------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | type        | supported_database_types                                     | description                | +-------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ | CRC32_MATCH | MySQL                                                        | Match CRC32 of records.    | | DATA_MATCH  | SQL92,MySQL,MariaDB,PostgreSQL,openGauss,Oracle,SQLServer,H2 | Match raw data of records. | +-------------+--------------------------------------------------------------+----------------------------+ 2 rows in set (0.03 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `MIGRATION`, `CHECK`, `ALGORITHMS`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-migration-show-migration-check-algorithm--related-links"></a>

SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHM PLUGINS

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--show-sharding-algorithm-plugins"></a>

# SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHM PLUGINS

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHM PLUGINS` syntax is used to query all the plugins of the interface `org.apache.shardingsphere.sharding.spi.ShardingAlgorithm`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
showShardingAlgorithmPlugins ::=
  'SHOW' 'SHARDING' 'ALGORITHM' 'PLUGINS'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| type | type |
| type\_aliases | type aliases |
| description | description |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--example"></a>

### Example

- Query all the plugins for `org.apache.shardingsphere.sharding.spi.ShardingAlgorithm` interface

```sql
SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHM PLUGINS
```

```sql
SHOW SHARDING ALGORITHM PLUGINS;
+----------------+--------------+-------------+
| type           | type_aliases | description |
+----------------+--------------+-------------+
| MOD            |              |             |
| HASH_MOD       |              |             |
| VOLUME_RANGE   |              |             |
| BOUNDARY_RANGE |              |             |
| AUTO_INTERVAL  |              |             |
| INTERVAL       |              |             |
| CLASS_BASED    |              |             |
| INLINE         |              |             |
| COMPLEX_INLINE |              |             |
| HINT_INLINE    |              |             |
+----------------+--------------+-------------+
10 rows in set (0.27 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-plugin-show-sharding-algorithm-plugins--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `SHARDING`, `ALGORITHM`, `PLUGINS`

SHOW COMPUTE NODE INFO

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--show-compute-node-info"></a>

# SHOW COMPUTE NODE INFO

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW COMPUTE NODE INFO` syntax is used to query current proxy instance information.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowComputeNodeInfo ::=
  'SHOW' 'COMPUTE' 'NODE' 'INFO'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| instance\_id | proxy instance id |
| host | host address |
| port | port number |
| status | proxy instance status |
| mode\_type | proxy instance mode |
| worker\_id | worker id |
| labels | labels |
| version | version |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--example"></a>

### Example

- Query current proxy instance information

```sql
SHOW COMPUTE NODE INFO;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW COMPUTE NODES;
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+
| instance_id                          | instance_type | host       | port | status | mode_type  | worker_id | labels | version  |
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+
| 3e84d33e-cb97-42f2-b6ce-f78fea0ded89 | PROXY         | 127.0.0.1  | 3307 | OK     | Cluster    | -1        |        | 5.4.2    |
+--------------------------------------+---------------+------------+------+--------+------------+-----------+--------+----------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `COMPUTE`, `NODE`, `INFO`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-info--related-links"></a>

Deep

Dark

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-compute-node-mode.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW COMPUTE NODE MODE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--show-compute-node-mode"></a>

# SHOW COMPUTE NODE MODE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW COMPUTE NODE MODE` syntax is used to query current proxy instance mode configuration information.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowComputeNodeMode ::=
  'SHOW' 'COMPUTE' 'NODE' 'MODE'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| type | type of proxy mode configuration |
| repository | type of persist repository |
| props | properties of persist repository |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--example"></a>

### Example

- Query current proxy instance mode configuration information

```sql
SHOW COMPUTE NODE MODE;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW COMPUTE NODE MODE; +---------+------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | type    | repository | props                                                                                                                                                                  | +---------+------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Cluster | ZooKeeper  | {"operationTimeoutMilliseconds":500,"timeToLiveSeconds":60,"maxRetries":3,"namespace":"governance_ds","server-lists":"localhost:2181","retryIntervalMilliseconds":500} | +---------+------------+------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ 1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `COMPUTE`, `NODE`, `MODE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-compute-node-mode--related-links"></a>

### Related links

# SHOW DIST VARIABLE

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-dist-variable.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW DIST VARIABLE

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--show-dist-variable"></a>

# SHOW DIST VARIABLE

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--return-value-description)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW DIST VARIABLE` syntax is used to query `PROXY` system variables configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowDistVariable ::=
  'SHOW' 'DIST' ('VARIABLES' ('LIKE' likePattern)?| 'VARIABLE' 'WHERE' 'NAME' '=' variableName)

likePattern ::=
  string

variableName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| variable\_name | system variable name |
| variable\_value | system variable value |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `variableName` is not specified, the default is query all `PROXY` variables configuration.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--example"></a>

### Example

- Query all system variables configuration of `PROXY`

```sql
SHOW DIST VARIABLES;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DIST VARIABLES;
+-----------------------------------------+-----------------+
| variable_name                           | variable_value  |
+-----------------------------------------+-----------------+
| agent_plugins_enabled                   | true            |
| cached_connections                      | 0               |
| cdc_server_port                         | 33071           |
| check_table_metadata_enabled            | false           |
| kernel_executor_size                    | 0               |
| load_table_metadata_batch_size          | 1000            |
| max_connections_size_per_query          | 1               |
| proxy_backend_query_fetch_size          | -1              |
| proxy_default_port                      | 3307            |
| proxy_frontend_database_protocol_type   |                 |
| proxy_frontend_executor_size            | 0               |
| proxy_frontend_flush_threshold          | 128             |
| proxy_frontend_max_connections          | 0               |
| proxy_frontend_ssl_cipher               |                 |
| proxy_frontend_ssl_enabled              | false           |
| proxy_frontend_ssl_version              | TLSv1.2,TLSv1.3 |
| proxy_meta_data_collector_enabled       | false           |
| proxy_netty_backlog                     | 1024            |
| sql_show                                | false           |
| sql_simple                              | false           |
| system_schema_metadata_assembly_enabled | true            |
+-----------------------------------------+-----------------+
21 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query specified system variable configuration of `PROXY`

```sql
SHOW DIST VARIABLE WHERE NAME = sql_show;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW DIST VARIABLE WHERE NAME = sql_show;
+---------------+----------------+
| variable_name | variable_value |
+---------------+----------------+
| sql_show      | false          |
+---------------+----------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `DIST`, `VARIABLE`, `VARIABLES`, `NAME`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-dist-variable--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/refresh-table-metadata/ -->

<!-- page_index: 260 -->

# REFRESH TABLE METADATA

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/refresh-table-metadata.en.md "Edit this page")

REFRESH TABLE METADATA

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--refresh-table-metadata"></a>

# REFRESH TABLE METADATA

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--description"></a>

### Description

The `REFRESH TABLE METADATA` syntax is used to refresh table metadata.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
RefreshTableMetadata ::=
  'REFRESH' 'TABLE' 'METADATA' (tableName | tableName 'FROM' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' storageUnitName ('SCHEMA' schemaName)?)?

tableName ::=
  distSQLIdentifier

storageUnitName ::=
  distSQLIdentifier

schemaName ::=
  distSQLIdentifier

distSQLIdentifier ::=
  identifier | quotedIdentifier

quotedIdentifier ::=
  '`' identifier '`' | '"' identifier '"'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `tableName` and `storageUnitName` is not specified, the default is to refresh all table metadata.
- refresh table metadata need to use `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.
- If there are no tables in the schema, the schema will be deleted.
- `tableName`, `storageUnitName`, and `schemaName` can be unquoted identifiers, backtick-quoted identifiers, or double-quoted identifiers.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--example"></a>

### Example

- Refresh specified table’s metadata in specified schema of a specified storage unit

```sql
REFRESH TABLE METADATA `t_order` FROM STORAGE UNIT "ds_1" SCHEMA `db_schema`;
```

- Refresh all tables' metadata in specified schema of a specified storage unit

```sql
REFRESH TABLE METADATA FROM STORAGE UNIT `ds_1` SCHEMA "db_schema";
```

- Refresh metadata for specified table in specified storage unit

```sql
REFRESH TABLE METADATA "t_order" FROM STORAGE UNIT ds_1;
```

- Refresh metadata for specified table

```sql
REFRESH TABLE METADATA `t_order`;
```

- Refresh all table metadata

```sql
REFRESH TABLE METADATA;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`REFRESH`, `TABLE`, `METADATA`, `FROM`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-table-metadata--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/refresh-database-metadata/ -->

<!-- page_index: 261 -->

# REFRESH DATABASE METADATA FROM GOVERNANCE CENTER

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/refresh-database-metadata.en.md "Edit this page")

REFRESH DATABASE METADATA FROM GOVERNANCE CENTER

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--refresh-database-metadata-from-governance-center"></a>

# REFRESH DATABASE METADATA FROM GOVERNANCE CENTER

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--description"></a>

### Description

The `REFRESH DATABASE METADATA` syntax is used to refresh the metadata of the local logic database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
RefreshDatabaseMetadata ::=
  'FORCE'? 'REFRESH' 'DATABASE' 'METADATA' databaseName?

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is to refresh all database metadata.
- When using `FORCE` to refresh metadata, the latest metadata will be obtained locally and written to the governance center. If without `FORCE`, it will be pulled from the governance center.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--example"></a>

### Example

- Refresh metadata for specified database

```sql
REFRESH DATABASE METADATA sharding_db;
```

- Refresh all database metadata

```sql
REFRESH DATABASE METADATA;
```

- Force refresh all database metadata

```sql
FORCE REFRESH DATABASE METADATA;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`FORCE`, `REFRESH`, `DATABASE`, `METADATA`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-refresh-database-metadata--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-table-metadata/ -->

<!-- page_index: 262 -->

# SHOW TABLE METADATA

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-table-metadata.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW TABLE METADATA

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--show-table-metadata"></a>

# SHOW TABLE METADATA

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--return-value-description)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW TABLE METADATA` syntax is used to query tabe metadata.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowTableMetadata ::=
  'SHOW' 'TABLE' 'METADATA' tableName (',' tableName)* ('FROM' databaseName)?

tableName ::=
  distSQLIdentifier

databaseName ::=
  distSQLIdentifier

distSQLIdentifier ::=
  identifier | quotedIdentifier

quotedIdentifier ::=
  '`' identifier '`' | '"' identifier '"'
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| schema\_name | database name |
| table\_name | table name |
| type | metadata type |
| name | metadata name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.
- `tableName` and `databaseName` can be unquoted identifiers, backtick-quoted identifiers, or double-quoted identifiers.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--example"></a>

### Example

- Query matadata of multiple tables from specified database

```sql
SHOW TABLE METADATA t_order, `t_order_1` FROM "sharding_db";
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TABLE METADATA t_order, `t_order_1` FROM "sharding_db"; +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | schema_name       | table_name | type   | name     | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order    | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ 8 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query metadata of one table from specified database

```sql
SHOW TABLE METADATA "t_order" FROM `sharding_db`;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TABLE METADATA "t_order" FROM `sharding_db`; +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | schema_name       | table_name | type   | name     | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order    | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ 4 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query metadata of multiple tables from current database

```sql
SHOW TABLE METADATA `t_order`, "t_order_1";
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TABLE METADATA `t_order`, "t_order_1"; +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | schema_name       | table_name | type   | name     | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order_1  | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order    | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ 8 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

- Query metadata of one table from current database

```sql
SHOW TABLE METADATA "t_order";
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW TABLE METADATA "t_order"; +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ | schema_name       | table_name | type   | name     | +-------------+------------+--------+----------+ | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | order_id | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | user_id  | | sharding_db       | t_order    | COLUMN | status   | | sharding_db       | t_order    | INDEX  | PRIMARY  | +-------------------+------------+--------+----------+ 4 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `TABLE`, `METADATA`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-table-metadata--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-rules-used-storage-unit/ -->

<!-- page_index: 263 -->

# SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/show-rules-used-storage-unit.en.md "Edit this page")

SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--show-rules-used-storage-unit"></a>

# SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--return-value-description)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--description"></a>

### Description

The `SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT` syntax is used to query the rules for using the specified storage unit in specified database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ShowRulesUsedStorageUnit ::=
  'SHOW' 'RULES' 'USED' 'STORAGE' 'UNIT' storageUnitName ('FROM' databaseName)?

storageUnitName ::=
  identifier

databaseName ::=
  identifier
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| type | rule type |
| name | rule name |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the default is the currently used `DATABASE`. If `DATABASE` is not used, `No database selected` will be prompted.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--example"></a>

### Example

- Query the rules for using the specified storage unit in specified database

```sql
SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT ds_1 FROM sharding_db;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT ds_1 FROM sharding_db; +---------------------+------------+ | type                | name       | +---------------------+------------+ | readwrite_splitting | ms_group_0 | | readwrite_splitting | ms_group_0 | +---------------------+------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Query the rules for using the specified storage unit in current database

```sql
SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT ds_1;
```

```sql
mysql> SHOW RULES USED STORAGE UNIT ds_1; +---------------------+------------+ | type                | name       | +---------------------+------------+ | readwrite_splitting | ms_group_0 | | readwrite_splitting | ms_group_0 | +---------------------+------------+ 2 rows in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`SHOW`, `RULES`, `USED`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`, `FROM`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-show-rules-used-storage-unit--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/export-database-configuration/ -->

<!-- page_index: 264 -->

# EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/export-database-configuration.en.md "Edit this page")

EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--export-database-configuration"></a>

# EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--description"></a>

### Description

The `EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION` syntax is used to export storage units and rule configurations to `YAML` format.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ExportDatabaseConfiguration ::=
  'EXPORT' 'DATABASE' 'CONFIGURATION' ('FROM' databaseName)? ('TO' 'FILE' filePath)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, the currently used logical database will be exported; if no database is used, `No database selected` will be prompted;
- When `filePath` is not specified, the exported information will be output through the result set;
- When `filePath` is specified, the file will be automatically created. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--example"></a>

### Example

- Export currently used logical database

```sql
mysql> EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION;
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| result                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| databaseName: sharding_db
dataSources:
  ds_1:
    password: 123456
    url: jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/db0
    username: root
    minPoolSize: 1
    connectionTimeoutMilliseconds: 30000
    maxLifetimeMilliseconds: 2100000
    readOnly: false
    idleTimeoutMilliseconds: 60000
    maxPoolSize: 50
  ds_2:
    password: 123456
    url: jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/db1
    username: root
    minPoolSize: 1
    connectionTimeoutMilliseconds: 30000
    maxLifetimeMilliseconds: 2100000
    readOnly: false
    idleTimeoutMilliseconds: 60000
    maxPoolSize: 50
rules:
 |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

- Export the specified logical database and output it to file

```sql
mysql> EXPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION FROM sharding_db TO FILE '/xxx/config_sharding_db.yaml';
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| result                                                                  |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Successfully exported to: '/xxx/config_sharding_db.yaml'  |
+-------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.02 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`EXPORT`, `DATABASE`, `CONFIGURATION`, `FROM`, `TO`, `FILE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-database-configuration--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/import-database-configuration/ -->

<!-- page_index: 265 -->

# IMPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/import-database-configuration.en.md "Edit this page")

IMPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--import-database-configuration"></a>

# IMPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--description"></a>

### Description

The `IMPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION` syntax is used to import a database from the configuration in `YAML`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ImportDatabaseConfiguration ::=
  'IMPORT' 'DATABASE' 'CONFIGURATION' 'FROM' 'FILE' filePath

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When a database with the same name already exists in the metadata, it cannot be imported;
- When `databaseName` in YAML is empty, it cannot be imported;
- When `dataSources` in YAML is empty, only empty database will be imported.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
IMPORT DATABASE CONFIGURATION FROM FILE "/xxx/config_sharding_db.yaml";
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`IMPORT`, `DATABASE`, `CONFIGURATION`, `FROM`, `FILE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-database-configuration--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/convert-yaml-configuration/ -->

<!-- page_index: 266 -->

# CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/convert-yaml-configuration.en.md "Edit this page")

CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--convert-yaml-configuration"></a>

# CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--description"></a>

### Description

The `CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION` syntax is used to convert `YAML` configuration to DistSQL RDL statements.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
convertYamlConfiguration ::=
  'CONVERT' 'YAML' 'CONFIGURATION' 'FROM' 'FILE' filePath

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- The `CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION` syntax only reads the YAML file and converts the configuration into DistSQL statements without affecting the current metadata;
- When `dataSources` in YAML is empty, `rules` conversion will not be performed.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
mysql> CONVERT YAML CONFIGURATION FROM FILE '/xxx/config_sharding_db.yaml'; +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | dist_sql                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | CREATE DATABASE sharding_db; USE sharding_db;
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (URL='jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/demo_ds_0?serverTimezone=UTC&useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true',USER='root',PASSWORD='123456',PROPERTIES('maxPoolSize'='10') ), ds_1 (URL='jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/demo_ds_1?serverTimezone=UTC&useSSL=false&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true',USER='root',PASSWORD='123456',PROPERTIES('maxPoolSize'='10') );
CREATE SHARDING TABLE RULE t_order (STORAGE_UNITS(ds_0,ds_1),SHARDING_COLUMN=order_id,TYPE(NAME='mod', PROPERTIES('sharding-count'='4')),KEY_GENERATE_STRATEGY(COLUMN=order_id, TYPE(NAME='snowflake')) );
| +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ 1 row in set (0.02 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`CONVERT`, `YAML`, `CONFIGURATION`, `FROM`, `FILE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-convert-yaml-configuration--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/lock-cluster-with/ -->

<!-- page_index: 267 -->

# LOCK CLUSTER WITH

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/lock-cluster-with.en.md "Edit this page")

LOCK CLUSTER WITH

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--lock-cluster-with"></a>

# LOCK CLUSTER WITH

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--example)
  - [Reserved words](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--reserved-words)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--description"></a>

### Description

The `LOCK CLUSTER WITH` syntax is utilized to apply a lock with a specific algorithm to the `CLUSTER`.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
LockClusterWith ::=
  'LOCK' 'CLUSTER' 'WITH' lockStrategy ('TIMEOUT' timeoutMillis)?

timeoutmillis ::=
  long
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When the `CLUSTER` is already locked, it is impossible to re-lock it, otherwise an exception will be thrown.
- Currently, the `lockStrategy` supports two lock strategies, namely the exclusive lock `WRITE` and the read-write lock `READ_WRITE` .
- The `timeoutMillis` is used to indicate the timeout period for attempting to acquire the lock, with the unit being milliseconds. When not specified, the default value is 3,000 milliseconds.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--example"></a>

### Example

- Lock the `CLUSTER` with an exclusive lock without setting the timeout.

```sql
LOCK CLUSTER WITH WRITE;
```

- Lock the CLUSTER with a read-write lock and set the timeout to 2000 milliseconds.

```sql
LOCK CLUSTER WITH READ_WRITE TIMEOUT 2000;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--reserved-words"></a>

### Reserved words

`LOCK`,`CLUSTER`,`WITH`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-lock-cluster-with--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/unlock-cluster/ -->

<!-- page_index: 268 -->

# UNLOCK CLUSTER

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/unlock-cluster.en.md "Edit this page")

UNLOCK CLUSTER

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--unlock-cluster"></a>

# UNLOCK CLUSTER

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--example)
  - [Reserved words](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--reserved-words)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--description"></a>

### Description

The `UNLOCK CLUSTER` syntax is used to release the lock applied to the `CLUSTER` by the `LOCK CLUSTER WITH` statement.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
UnlockCluster ::=
  'UNLOCK' 'CLUSTER' ('TIMEOUT' timeoutMillis)?

timeoutmillis ::=
  long
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When the `CLUSTER` is not in a locked state, it is impossible to release the lock; otherwise, an exception will be thrown.
- `timeoutMillis` is used to indicate the timeout duration for attempting to unlock, with the unit being milliseconds. When not specified, the default value is 3,000 milliseconds.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--example"></a>

### Example

- Unlock the `CLUSTER` without setting a timeout.

```sql
UNLOCK CLUSTER;
```

- Unlock the `CLUSTER` and set the timeout to 2000 milliseconds.

```sql
UNLOCK CLUSTER TIMEOUT 2000;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--reserved-words"></a>

### Reserved words

`UNLOCK`,`CLUSTER`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-unlock-cluster--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/export-metadata/ -->

<!-- page_index: 269 -->

# EXPORT METADATA

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/export-metadata.en.md "Edit this page")

EXPORT METADATA

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--export-metadata"></a>

# EXPORT METADATA

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--return-value-description)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--description"></a>

### Description

The `EXPORT METADATA` syntax is used to export cluster metadata in `JSON` format.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ExportMetaData ::=
  'EXPORT' 'METADATA' ('TO' 'FILE' filePath)?

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | current compute node id |
| create\_time | export time |
| cluster\_info | exported metadata (or success message when `TO FILE` is set) |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `filePath` is not specified, the `cluster_info` column returns Base64-encoded metadata;
- When `filePath` is specified, metadata is written to file and `cluster_info` returns a success message;
- The parent directory of `filePath` will be created automatically if needed, and existing file content will be overwritten.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
EXPORT METADATA;
```

```sql
EXPORT METADATA TO FILE '/tmp/metadata.json';
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`EXPORT`, `METADATA`, `TO`, `FILE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-metadata--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/import-metadata/ -->

<!-- page_index: 270 -->

# IMPORT METADATA

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/import-metadata.en.md "Edit this page")

IMPORT METADATA

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--import-metadata"></a>

# IMPORT METADATA

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--syntax)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--description"></a>

### Description

The `IMPORT METADATA` syntax is used to import cluster metadata from file or inline value.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ImportMetaData ::=
  'IMPORT' 'METADATA' (metaDataValue | 'FROM' 'FILE' filePath)

metaDataValue ::=
  string

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- `metaDataValue` should be a Base64 string exported by `EXPORT METADATA`;
- `FROM FILE` should point to a JSON file exported by `EXPORT METADATA TO FILE`;
- If the imported metadata contains an existing logical database, import will fail;
- If the metadata file does not exist or content format is invalid, import will fail.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
IMPORT METADATA FROM FILE '/tmp/metadata.json';
```

```sql
IMPORT METADATA 'eyJtZXRhX2RhdGEiOns...';
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-import-metadata--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`IMPORT`, `METADATA`, `FROM`, `FILE`
