<!-- page_index: 271 -->

# EXPORT STORAGE NODES

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/ral/export-storage-nodes.en.md "Edit this page")

EXPORT STORAGE NODES

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--export-storage-nodes"></a>

# EXPORT STORAGE NODES

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--return-value-description)
  - [Supplement](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--supplement)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--description"></a>

### Description

The `EXPORT STORAGE NODES` syntax is used to export storage node configurations in `JSON` format.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ExportStorageNodes ::=
  'EXPORT' 'STORAGE' 'NODES' ('FROM' databaseName)? ('TO' 'FILE' filePath)?

databaseName ::=
  identifier

filePath ::=
  string
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Columns | Description |
| --- | --- |
| id | current compute node id |
| create\_time | export time |
| storage\_nodes | exported storage node JSON (or success message when `TO FILE` is set) |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--supplement"></a>

### Supplement

- When `databaseName` is not specified, storage nodes of all logical databases are exported;
- When `databaseName` is specified, only storage nodes of the specified logical database are exported;
- If the specified `databaseName` does not exist, export will fail;
- When `filePath` is specified, the result is written to file and existing file content will be overwritten;
- Exported JSON contains connection information such as `username` and `password`; protect the output properly.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--example"></a>

### Example

```sql
EXPORT STORAGE NODES;
```

```sql
EXPORT STORAGE NODES FROM sharding_db TO FILE '/tmp/storage-nodes.json';
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`EXPORT`, `STORAGE`, `NODES`, `FROM`, `TO`, `FILE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-ral-export-storage-nodes--related-links"></a>

### Related links

---

# PARSE SQL

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/syntax/rul/parse-sql.en.md "Edit this page")

PARSE SQL

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--parse-sql"></a>

# PARSE SQL

- - [Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--description)
  - [Syntax](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--syntax)
  - [Return Value Description](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--return-value-description)
  - [Example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--example)
  - [Reserved word](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--reserved-word)
  - [Related links](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--related-links)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--description"></a>

### Description

The `PARSE SQL` syntax is used to parse `SQL` and output abstract syntax tree.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
ParseSql ::=
  'PARSE' sqlStatement  
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| parsed\_statement | parsed SQL statement type |
| parsed\_statement\_detail | detail of the parsed statement |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--example"></a>

### Example

- Parse `SQL` and output abstract syntax tree

```sql
PARSE SELECT * FROM t_order;
```

```sql
mysql> PARSE SELECT * FROM t_order;
+----------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| parsed_statement     | parsed_statement_detail                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
+----------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| SelectStatement      | {"projections":{"startIndex":7,"stopIndex":7,"projections":[{"startIndex":7,"stopIndex":7}],"distinctRow":false},"from":{"tableName":{"startIndex":14,"stopIndex":20,"identifier":{"value":"t_order","quoteCharacter":"NONE"}}},"parameterCount":0,"parameterMarkerSegments":[],"commentSegments":[]} |
+----------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--reserved-word"></a>

### Reserved word

`PARSE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-parse-sql--related-links"></a>

### Related links

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-format-sql--description"></a>

### Description

The `FORMAT SQL` syntax is used to parse `SQL` and output formatted `SQL` statement.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-format-sql--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
FormatSql ::=
  'FORMAT' sqlStatement  
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-format-sql--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| formatted\_result | formatted SQL statement |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-format-sql--example"></a>

### Example

- Parse `SQL` and output formatted `SQL` statement

```sql
FORMAT SELECT * FROM t_order;
```

```sql
mysql> FORMAT SELECT * FROM t_order; +-------------------------+ | formatted_result        | +-------------------------+ | SELECT * FROM t_order; | +-------------------------+ 1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-format-sql--reserved-word"></a>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-preview-sql--description"></a>

### Description

The `PREVIEW SQL` syntax is used to preview `SQL` execution plan.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-preview-sql--syntax"></a>

### Syntax

Grammar
Railroad diagram

```sql
PreviewSql ::=
  'PREVIEW' sqlStatement  
```

<iframe frameborder="0" height="100%" id="diagram" name="diagram" width="100%"></iframe>

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-preview-sql--return-value-description"></a>

### Return Value Description

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| data\_source\_name | storage unit name |
| actual\_sql | actual excute SQL statement |

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-preview-sql--example"></a>

### Example

- Preview `SQL` execution plan

```sql
PREVIEW SELECT * FROM t_order;
```

```sql
mysql> PREVIEW SELECT * FROM t_order;
+------------------+-----------------------+
| data_source_name | actual_sql            |
+------------------+-----------------------+
| su_1             | SELECT * FROM t_order |
+------------------+-----------------------+
1 row in set (0.18 sec)
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-rul-preview-sql--reserved-word"></a>

, `IGNORE`, `SINGLE`, `TABLES`

#### Sharding

, `DATANODES`, `DATABASE_STRATEGY`, `TABLE_STRATEGY`, `KEY_GENERATE_STRATEGY`, `RESOURCES`, `SHARDING_COLUMN`, `KEY`
, `GENERATOR`, `SHARDING_COLUMNS`, `KEY_GENERATOR`, `SHARDING_ALGORITHM`, `COLUMN`, `AUDIT_STRATEGY`
, `AUDITORS`, `ALLOW_HINT_DISABLE`

`BROADCAST`

#### Readwrite-Splitting

`READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `WRITE_STORAGE_UNIT`, `READ_STORAGE_UNITS`
, `AUTO_AWARE_RESOURCE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-reserved-word--encrypt"></a>

#### Encrypt

`ENCRYPT`, `COLUMNS`, `CIPHER`, `ENCRYPT_ALGORITHM`

`DB_DISCOVERY`, `STORAGE_UNITS`, `HEARTBEAT`

`SHADOW`, `DEFAULT`, `SOURCE`, `SHADOW`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-reserved-word--mask"></a>

#### MASK

`MASK`, `COLUMNS`

## RAL

`ALTER`, `READWRITE_SPLITTING`, `RULE`, `RULES`, `FROM`, `ENABLE`, `DISABLE`, `SHOW`, `COMPUTE`, `NODES`, `NODE`
, `STATUS`, `LABEL`, `RELABEL`, `WITH`, `UNLABEL`, `AUTHORITY`, `TRANSACTION`, `SQL_PARSER`, `SQL_TRANSLATOR`, `DEFAULT`, `TYPE`
, `NAME`, `PROPERTIES`, `USE_ORIGINAL_SQL_WHEN_TRANSLATING_FAILED`, `PARSE_TREE_CACHE`, `INITIAL_CAPACITY`, `MAXIMUM_SIZE`
, `CONCURRENCY_LEVEL`, `SQL_STATEMENT_CACHE`, `TRAFFIC`, `TRAFFIC_ALGORITHM`, `LOAD_BALANCER`, `CREATE`
, `DATABASE_VALUE`, `TABLE_VALUE`, `CLEAR`, `MIGRATION`, `READ`, `WRITE`, `WORKER_THREAD`, `BATCH_SIZE`
, `SHARDING_SIZE`, `STREAM_CHANNEL`, `REGISTER`, `URL`, `UNREGISTER`, `UNITS`, `INTO`, `LIST`, `CHECK`, `BY`
, `STOP`, `START`, `ROLLBACK`, `COMMIT`, `INFO`, `MODE`, `DIST`, `VARIABLE`, `VARIABLES`, `WHERE`, `DROPSET`
, `SET`, `HINT`, `SOURCE`, `ADD`, `SHARDING`, `STORAGE`, `UNIT`, `USER`, `PASSWORD`, `REFRESH`, `METADATA`, `TABLE`
, `DATABASE`, `GOVERNANCE`, `CENTER`, `EXPORT`, `CONFIGURATION`, `TO`, `FILE`, `IMPORT`, `USED`, `IMPLEMENTATIONS`, `OF`
, `KEY`, `GENERATE`, `ALGORITHM`, `QUERY`, `LOAD`, `BALANCE`, `FEDERATION`, `SQL_FEDERATION_ENABLED`
, `ALL_QUERY_USE_SQL_FEDERATION`, `EXECUTION_PLAN_CACHE`

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-syntax-reserved-word--rul"></a>

## RUL

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/ -->

<!-- page_index: 277 -->

# Usage :: ShardingSphere

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/_index.en.md "Edit this page")

Usage

- [Pre-work](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--pre-work)
- [Create Logic Database](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--create-logic-database)
- [Resource Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--resource-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--rule-operation)

This chapter will introduce how to use DistSQL to manage resources and rules in a distributed database.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--pre-work"></a>

## Pre-work

Use MySQL as example, can replace to other databases.

1. Start the MySQL service;
2. Create to be registered MySQL databases;
3. Create role and user in MySQL with creation permission for ShardingSphere-Proxy;
4. Start Zookeeper service;
5. Add `mode` and `authentication` configurations to `global.yaml`;
6. Start ShardingSphere-Proxy;
7. Use SDK or terminal connect to ShardingSphere-Proxy.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--create-logic-database"></a>

## Create Logic Database

1. Create logic database

```sql
CREATE DATABASE foo_db;
```

2. Use newly created logic database

```sql
USE foo_db;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--resource-operation"></a>

## Resource Operation

More details please see concentrate rule examples.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

More details please see concentrate rule examples.

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage--notice"></a>

> [!NOTE]
> ### Notice

1. Currently, `DROP DATABASE` will only remove the `logical distributed database`, not the user’s actual database;
2. `DROP TABLE` will delete all logical fragmented tables and actual tables in the database;
3. `CREATE DATABASE` will only create a `logical distributed database`, so users need to create actual databases in advance.

---

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/sharding-rule/ -->

<!-- page_index: 278 -->

# Sharding

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/sharding-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

Sharding

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule--sharding"></a>

# Sharding

- [Storage unit Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule--storage-unit-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule--rule-operation)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule--storage-unit-operation"></a>

## Storage unit Operation

- Configure data source information

```sql
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_1",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
),ds_1 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_2",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-sharding-rule--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

- Create sharding rule

```sql
CREATE SHARDING TABLE RULE t_order(
STORAGE_UNITS(ds_0,ds_1),
SHARDING_COLUMN=order_id,
TYPE(NAME="hash_mod",PROPERTIES("sharding-count"="4")),
KEY_GENERATE_STRATEGY(COLUMN=order_id,TYPE(NAME="snowflake"))
);
```

- Create sharding table

```sql
CREATE TABLE `t_order` (
  `order_id` int NOT NULL,
  `user_id` int NOT NULL,
  `status` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`order_id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4
```

- Drop sharding table

```sql
DROP TABLE t_order;
```

- Drop sharding rule

```sql
DROP SHARDING TABLE RULE t_order;
```

- Unregister storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0, ds_1;
```

- Drop distributed database

```sql
DROP DATABASE foo_db;
```

---

<!-- source_url: https://shardingsphere.apache.org/document/current/en/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/readwirte-splitting-rule/ -->

<!-- page_index: 279 -->

# Readwrite_splitting

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/readwirte-splitting-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

Readwrite\_splitting

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-readwirte-splitting-rule--readwrite_splitting"></a>

# Readwrite\_splitting

- [Storage unit Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-readwirte-splitting-rule--storage-unit-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-readwirte-splitting-rule--rule-operation)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-readwirte-splitting-rule--storage-unit-operation"></a>

## Storage unit Operation

```sql
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT write_ds (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_0",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
),read_ds (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3307,
    DB="ds_0",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-readwirte-splitting-rule--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

- Create readwrite\_splitting rule

```sql
CREATE READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE group_0 (
WRITE_STORAGE_UNIT=write_ds,
READ_STORAGE_UNITS(read_ds),
TYPE(NAME="random")
);
```

- Alter readwrite\_splitting rule

```sql
ALTER READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE group_0 (
WRITE_STORAGE_UNIT=write_ds,
READ_STORAGE_UNITS(read_ds),
TYPE(NAME="random",PROPERTIES("read_weight"="2:0"))
);
```

- Drop readwrite\_splitting rule

```sql
DROP READWRITE_SPLITTING RULE group_0;
```

- Unregister storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER STORAGE UNIT write_ds,read_ds;
```

- Drop distributed database

```sql
DROP DATABASE readwrite_splitting_db;
```

---

<!-- page_index: 280 -->

# Encrypt

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/encrypt-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

Encrypt

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-encrypt-rule--encrypt"></a>

# Encrypt

- [Storage unit Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-encrypt-rule--storage-unit-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-encrypt-rule--rule-operation)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-encrypt-rule--storage-unit-operation"></a>

## Storage unit Operation

```sql
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_0",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-encrypt-rule--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

- Create encrypt rule

```sql
CREATE ENCRYPT RULE t_encrypt (
    COLUMNS(
        (NAME=user_id,CIPHER=user_cipher,ENCRYPT_ALGORITHM(TYPE(NAME='AES',PROPERTIES('aes-key-value'='123456abc', 'digest-algorithm-name'='SHA-1')))),
        (NAME=order_id,CIPHER =order_cipher,ENCRYPT_ALGORITHM(TYPE(NAME='AES',PROPERTIES('aes-key-value'='123456abc', 'digest-algorithm-name'='SHA-1'))))
));
```

- Create encrypt table

```sql
CREATE TABLE `t_encrypt` (
    `id` int(11) NOT NULL,
    `user_id` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
    `order_id` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
```

- Alter encrypt rule

```sql
ALTER ENCRYPT RULE t_encrypt (
    COLUMNS(
        (NAME=user_id,CIPHER=user_cipher,ENCRYPT_ALGORITHM(TYPE(NAME='AES',PROPERTIES('aes-key-value'='123456abc', 'digest-algorithm-name'='SHA-1'))))
));
```

- Drop encrypt rule

```sql
DROP ENCRYPT RULE t_encrypt;
```

- Unregister storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0;
```

- Drop distributed database

```sql
DROP DATABASE encrypt_db;
```

# MASK

Retro

Eye care

Haitian

Deep

Dark

Default

[Edit this page](https://github.com/apache/shardingsphere/tree/master/docs/document/content/user-manual/shardingsphere-proxy/distsql/usage/mask-rule.en.md "Edit this page")

MASK

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-mask-rule--mask"></a>

# MASK

- [Storage unit Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-mask-rule--storage-unit-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-mask-rule--rule-operation)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-mask-rule--storage-unit-operation"></a>

## Storage unit Operation

```sql
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_0",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-mask-rule--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

- Create mask rule

```sql
CREATE MASK RULE t_mask (
    COLUMNS(
        (NAME=phone_number,TYPE(NAME='MASK_FROM_X_TO_Y', PROPERTIES("from-x"=1, "to-y"=2, "replace-char"="*"))),
        (NAME=address,TYPE(NAME='MD5'))
));
```

- Create mask table

```sql
CREATE TABLE `t_mask` (
    `id` int(11) NOT NULL,
    `user_id` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
    `phone_number` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
    `address` varchar(45) DEFAULT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
```

- Alter mask rule

```sql
ALTER MASK RULE t_mask (
    COLUMNS(
        (NAME=user_id,TYPE(NAME='MD5'))
));
```

- Drop mask rule

```sql
DROP MASK RULE t_mask;
```

- Unregister storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0;
```

- Drop distributed database

```sql
DROP DATABASE mask_db;
```

Deep

Shadow

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-shadow-rule--shadow"></a>

# Shadow

- [Storage unit Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-shadow-rule--storage-unit-operation)
- [Rule Operation](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-shadow-rule--rule-operation)
<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-shadow-rule--storage-unit-operation"></a>

## Storage unit Operation

```sql
REGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_0",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
),ds_1 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_1",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
),ds_2 (
    HOST="127.0.0.1",
    PORT=3306,
    DB="ds_2",
    USER="root",
    PASSWORD="root"
);
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-distsql-usage-shadow-rule--rule-operation"></a>

## Rule Operation

- Create shadow rule

```sql
CREATE SHADOW RULE group_0(
SOURCE=ds_0,
SHADOW=ds_1,
t_order(TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT"),TYPE(NAME="REGEX_MATCH", PROPERTIES("operation"="insert","column"="user_id", "regex"='[1]'))), 
t_order_item(TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT")));
```

- Alter shadow rule

```sql
ALTER SHADOW RULE group_0(
SOURCE=ds_0,
SHADOW=ds_2,
t_order_item(TYPE(NAME="SQL_HINT")));
```

- Drop shadow rule

```sql
DROP SHADOW RULE group_0;
```

- Unregister storage unit

```sql
UNREGISTER STORAGE UNIT ds_0,ds_1,ds_2;
```

- Drop distributed database

```sql
DROP DATABASE foo_db;
```

<a id="user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-migration-usage--manual"></a>

# Manual

- [MySQL user guide](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-migration-usage--mysql-user-guide)

  - [Complete procedure example](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-migration-usage--complete-procedure-example-1)
- [openGauss user guide](#user-manual-shardingsphere-proxy-migration-usage--opengauss-user-guide)
